- [O4Z-AUDIT-RUB] - Deep audit `remove-unused-brs`
  - Status: current v0.1.0 release-gating `-O4z` per-pass audit closed under the RUB-Q approved-substitute clause plus RUB-R through RUB-X follow-up closeouts. 2026-06-18 behavior analysis against the Binaryen phase model in `docs/wiki/binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/binaryen-strategy.md` and the local inventory in `docs/wiki/binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/pattern-catalog.md` found Starshine covered the core tail/return cleanup, constant/payload `br_if`, many value-`if`/`select`, local-set, carried-wrapper, branch-payload, `br_table` continuation-wrapper, safe switch cleanup, loop cleanup, branch-to-trap, EH caught-throw cleanup, and no-op skip families, but not the full upstream staged surface. Follow-up slices through 2026-06-29 either implemented safe GC/block/jump-thread/final-optimizer/table/dead-shell improvements or narrowed the remaining metadata/pass-option, descriptor-BrOn, ChildLocalizer/scratch-local, public-stack BrOn, and result-suffix surfaces to precise blockers with reopening criteria.
  - Scope: tail branch/return removal, constant/payload `br_if`, branch-to-trap rewrite, value-if/select lowering, br_table labels, raw gate thresholds, EH caught-throw cleanup, GC `br_on_*` cleanup, branch-hint/never-unconditionalize behavior, switch cleanup, loop/block reshaping, jump-threading, and retired slot14/slot40 corruption families.
  - Deliverables: apply the common checklist; coordinate with `[AUDIT002-B]` through `[AUDIT002-E]`; refresh direct compare and all `RUB` slot evidence; measure pass-local impact of raw skip choices; use `docs/wiki/raw/research/0721-2026-06-09-remove-unused-brs-merge-blocks-audit.md` as the latest merge-blocks/fallback evidence.
  - Missing Binaryen behavior slices from the 2026-06-18 audit:
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-A]` Source-refresh and shape matrix for Binaryen `RemoveUnusedBrs.cpp` at the current local oracle version. Completed 2026-06-18: local `wasm-opt --version` is `version_130`; re-read `RemoveUnusedBrs.cpp`, `branch-utils.h`, `branch-hints.h`, `cost.h`, `drop.h`, `effects.h`, `gc-type-utils.h`, `localize.h`, `properties.h`, and the full official `remove-unused-brs*` lit roster; filed the source note at `docs/wiki/raw/binaryen/2026-06-18-remove-unused-brs-version-130-source-refresh.md`; updated the RUB matrix in `docs/wiki/binaryen/passes/remove-unused-brs/implementation-structure-and-tests.md`; recorded the Wasm 3.0 baseline rule that GC is not optional/gated for this audit unless a local tool/parser blocker is documented.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-B]` Early `optimizeSwitch` parity safe subset. Completed 2026-06-18: added test-first coverage and implementation for no-payload trailing-default trimming, leading-default offsetting, default-only `br_table` lowering, one-explicit-target/two-option branch-if lowering, and large mostly-default nested stack-style lowering through a narrow O4z raw-gate exception into the existing HOT switch optimizer. Follow-up value-carrying target cleanup is also complete: trailing-default trimming and leading-default selector offsets preserve payload children and branch arity, while default-only/two-option value tables stay as `br_table` forms at Binaryen's value-sensitive bailout boundary. Explicit blocker remains documented in the RUB docs: child-less local stack-payload switch shapes stay conservative because ordinary lifted value switches carry payloads as children.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-C]` Full loop cleanup parity beyond current block/loop rotation. Completed 2026-06-18: Starshine now covers Binaryen `optimizeLoop(...)` loop bodies ending in simple `br $loop` for adjacent exit-driving `br_if` (flip condition and retarget the backedge), single-use block-exit `br_if` with intervening void suffix movement into the fallthrough/else arm, and the pre-existing `if` suffix-movement families. Focused negatives keep multi-use block-exit targets, intervening control transfer, and nested value-control hazards conservative.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-D]` Binaryen-style `sinkBlocks` safe subset. Completed 2026-06-19: loop-wrapper rotation was already covered by `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-C]`; this slice added red-first coverage and HOT support for void named blocks whose sole child is an `if`, sinking the block into the one multi-root arm that uses the block label when the condition and opposite arm do not. Condition-label-use and both-arm-use negatives stay conservative, and single-root branch-tail arms remain delegated to existing one-arm/self-branch cleanup rather than this sinker. Remaining reopening criteria: result-typed block/if sinks, unreachable-condition sink-specific assertions where DCE does not erase the shape first, and exact Binaryen branch-hint/refinalization metadata behavior.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-E]` EH caught-`throw` to branch cleanup. Completed 2026-06-18: added red-first `try_table` coverage and HOT implementation for Binaryen `visitThrow(...)` safe cases. Exact tag catches rewrite `throw` to `br` while preserving payload children; `catch_all` without refs drops payload children before branching. Tag mismatches, `catch_ref`, `catch_all_ref`, and earlier catch-ref-before-catch-all exnref transport stay explicit negatives. Legacy old-`try` mixed-control remains a local public-pipeline blocker because WAT lowering desugars legacy `try` before HOT; if a binary-decoder path exposes HOT `Try`, the matcher remains conservative.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-F]` GC `br_on_*` cleanup safe subset. Completed 2026-06-19: added red-first binary-AST coverage and HOT/raw support for locally provable Binaryen `optimizeGC` cases with a single ref child: definitely-not-taken `br_on_null`, definitely-taken `br_on_null`, definitely-taken `br_on_non_null`, definitely-not-taken `br_on_non_null`, definitely-successful `br_on_cast`, and definitely-not-taken `br_on_cast_fail`. Payload-bearing BrOn forms, nullable-cast success-only-if-non-null splitting, descriptor variants (`br_on_cast_desc_eq*`), broader fallthrough-type/local.tee cast insertion, and unreachable-input dropped-child construction are explicit fail-closed boundaries with reopening criteria in the RUB dossier.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-G]` General JumpThreader safe subset. Completed 2026-06-19: added red-first coverage and HOT retargeting for no-payload `br_if` branches through one-child named block shells and child blocks followed by a simple jump, matching the `version_130` relaxed one-child block behavior for the conditional no-payload subset. Existing `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-M]` covers child-to-`unreachable` direct `br` trap rewriting. The older reopening list is now narrowed by `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-Q]`: direct unconditional `br` and small/medium no-payload `br_table` retargeting are implemented, payload sent-type JumpThreader retargeting is a Binaryen `version_130` non-goal, and catch/large-table/non-payload `replacePossibleTarget` parity remain explicit reopening criteria.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-H]` Late adjacent `br_if` merge parity. Completed 2026-06-19: added shrink-mode-aware O4z coverage and HOT support for no-payload adjacent same-target `br_if` merging with `i32.or` when the later condition is locally safe to speculate. The matcher handles child-form and lifted stack-form branch conditions, rejects target mismatches and effectful later conditions, and pierces the broad O4z raw no-op gate only for simple stack-condition candidates. Payload/value branches, broad Binaryen cost/effect modeling, adjacent `br_if` + unconditional `br` cleanup, `never-unconditionalize`, and branch-hint `applyOrTo` metadata remain explicit follow-ups under `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-N]` / later final-optimizer slices because Starshine currently has no RUB branch-hint metadata representation.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-I]` Full `tablify` parity. Completed 2026-06-19: widened the equality-ladder `br_table` rewrite to Binaryen's dense-run rules for no-payload `br_if` roots, including `i32.eqz` as constant zero, local.tee-to-local.get selector sharing, distinct branch targets, unique constants, `MIN_NUM=3`, `MAX_RANGE=1024`, `range <= arms * 3`, min-offset selector subtraction, and wrapper default-label construction. Focused negatives keep duplicate constants, sparse ranges, negative / too-large constants, selector mismatches, and payload/value branches conservative.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-J]` Full `restructureIf` parity. Completed 2026-06-19: added HOT support for Binaryen-style named blocks whose leading self-target `br_if` exits the block and has no other target uses. Void forms rebuild to an outer `if (i32.eqz condition)`; dropped value forms rebuild to result `if` when the value is locally reorder-safe, or to `select` when the value must execute and the fallthrough arm is pure. Focused negatives keep additional target uses and effectful fallthrough arms. Branch-hint metadata and `never-unconditionalize` policy remain explicit `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-N]` boundaries.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-K]` Full `selectify` parity. Completed 2026-06-19: direct value-`if` selectification now applies Binaryen-shaped unconditionalization cost thresholds for HOT direct `selectify` candidates, keeping costly integer div/rem and trapping int-convert arms as `if`s at speed shrink level while allowing the same one-costly-arm shape under `shrink_level=1`; existing guards continue to require one-result, emit-select-compatible scalar arms, reorder/effect safety, and local-read safety for nonreorder conditions. Branch-hint metadata and `remove-unused-brs-never-unconditionalize` remain the explicit `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-N]` unsupported boundary.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-L]` Complete `optimizeSetIf` parity. Completed 2026-06-19: extended the local-set arm cleanup to the locally representable Binaryen `optimizeSetIf(...)` shapes. Copy-arm removal now handles either arm and `local.tee` result-block preservation; branch-arm extraction now handles either arm, preserves surviving `local.tee` results, flips conditions with `i32.eqz`, and re-enters the region pass so newly exposed nested set-if copy arms clean up recursively. Branch-hint metadata and `never-unconditionalize` behavior remain explicit `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-N]` boundaries.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-M]` Branch-to-trap parity. Completed 2026-06-18: added source-backed coverage from `remove-unused-brs_trap.wast` for simple branches targeting a void block whose containing region immediately falls into `unreachable`, including a `br_table` dispatch case where the table target remains intact and only the direct branch becomes `unreachable`; conditional `br_if` to the trap block stays conservative like Binaryen's JumpThreader. Implementation lives in `remove_unused_brs_try_rewrite_branches_to_trap_block(...)` and does not widen raw gates or broad nested traversal.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-N]` Branch-hints and `never-unconditionalize` contract. Completed 2026-06-19 as a source/docs boundary slice: Starshine has no local expression-level branch-hint representation, parser/lowerer path, binary code-metadata section, or RUB remap target, so Binaryen `BranchHints::copyTo`, `copyFlippedTo`, `applyAndTo`, `applyOrTo`, `flip`, and `clear` are documented as unsupported metadata preservation rather than hidden parity. Binaryen `remove-unused-brs-never-unconditionalize` is also unsupported locally because Starshine has no `--pass-arg` surface or pass option for it; all RUB rewrites that would need the flag are documented as ordinary direct-pass behavior until a public pass-arg/option exists. Reopen with an expression-code-metadata representation plus parser/lowerer/binary/remap tests and explicit RUB pass-option tests.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-O]` Direct and O4z slot signoff after the behavior slices. Completed 2026-06-19 after RUB-P: focused/full Moon tests and native `src/cmd` build passed during the RUB-P slice; post-RUB-P final direct 100000 compared `99751/100000` with `2` remaining raw mismatches classified by agent judgment as Starshine-win pure dropped-expression removals before unconditional trap/branch (`case-027803-wasm-smith`, `case-048903-wasm-smith`) and `249` Binaryen/tool command failures (`219` rec-group-zero, `12` bad-section-size, `11` command-failed, `6` table-index-out-of-range, `1` invalid-tag-index). Generated O4z slot14/slot40 full and extracted direct replays validate with `wasm-tools` from `.tmp/rub-o-post-rub-p-slots`. RUB-N/RUB-X remain the documented unsupported metadata/pass-option boundary.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-P]` Late `visitSwitch` one-target collapse parity. Completed 2026-06-19: Starshine now covers the local HOT subset of Binaryen `FinalOptimizer::visitSwitch(...)` for lifted value-carrying one-target `br_table`s, rewriting to `drop(selector); br payload` when the selector is const/local.get and payload values are locally reorder-safe. Focused coverage locks the local-selector positive and an effectful call/call reorder negative; child-less stack-payload switches and broader effect/reorder pairs remain conservative. Evidence: focused RUB tests passed, `moon test src/passes` and full `moon test` passed, native `src/cmd` release build passed, and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-remove-unused-brs-rub-p-10000` compared `9977/10000` with `0` mismatches and `23` Binaryen/tool command failures (`22` rec-group-zero, `1` bad-section-size).

Goal: recursively revisit every remaining Binaryen `RemoveUnusedBrs.cpp` family that the current dossier still marks as missing, partial, conservative, or representation-blocked; implement each source-backed safe family with red-first focused coverage, or narrow/document the blocker with exact reopening criteria. Completed in RUB-Q so far: narrow no-payload direct childless-`br` JumpThreader retargeting through one-child named block shells when reached through ordinary body/child traversal; no-payload `br_table` target/default retargeting through one-child named block shells and child-block-to-following-simple-jump shapes via `hot_branch_table_set(...)` for zero-child or one-selector-child HOT tables up to nine explicit targets, with the nine-target guard backed by `1375` and larger mostly-default switch interactions still blocked; audited `if`-arm JumpThreader behavior where direct no-payload branches are already covered via branch-exit/`br_if` normalization and small child-form no-payload `br_table` targets now retarget through `if` then/else regions; stack-source WAT table probes whose former inner/child target now retargets after local lowering to selector-child HOT tables; explicit value-carrying direct `br`, `br_if`, `br_table`, stack-source value-carrying table, and value-carrying if-arm table boundary coverage, with payload JumpThreader retargeting documented as a Binaryen `version_130` non-goal because the upstream collector records only `Type::none` sent-type uses; no-payload `try_table` catch-target retargeting for the child-block-to-following-simple-jump shape, with `catch_ref` boundary coverage; no-payload `try_table` catch-target retargeting through one-child named block shells after a narrow raw-gate admission plus HOT catch-label retarget helper; the no-payload nullable-source/non-null-target `br_on_cast` `SuccessOnlyIfNonNull` subset, lowering it to `br_on_non_null` plus appended `ref.null` while preserving the old drop-context shell; the no-payload non-null disjoint-family `br_on_cast` / `br_on_cast_fail` definite-failure subset, with nullable disjoint `SuccessOnlyIfNull` documented as a Binaryen `version_130` TODO boundary; the safe branch-taking GC prefix-payload subset for definitely taken `br_on_null` / `br_on_non_null`, definitely successful `br_on_cast`, and definitely failing non-null disjoint `br_on_cast_fail`; a focused fallthrough-producing stack-payload `SuccessOnlyIfNonNull` `br_on_cast` boundary proving Binaryen needs `ChildLocalizer`/scratch-local repair to feed the inner `br_on_non_null` and still drop the payload on fallthrough; child-form ordinary `br_on_cast*` unreachable-input cleanup that drops payload children before the replacement `unreachable`, with descriptor `br_on_cast_desc_eq*` kept as a representation blocker and public stack-form unreachable-input cleanup kept as a candidate-lowering/raw-proof boundary; the one-result and multi-result `sinkBlocks` subsets for matching block/if result types, including HOT repeated-root span repair for multivalue roots; the no-payload adjacent `br_if` + unconditional same-target `br` cleanup for child-form and stack-form HOT shapes, with a value-carrying adjacent-merge boundary test backed by Binaryen's no-value asserts; the local repeated-`local.get`/constant subset of Binaryen's self-target redundant `br_if` value cleanup, with a `local.tee` invalidation boundary from note `1377`; the final `selectify(...)`, `restructureIf(...)`, and `optimizeSetIf(...)` value-legality boundary audit from note `1378`, including condition-invalidated selectification, invalidated dropped-prefix rest, conditional `br_if` set-if arm coverage, and an unreachable-condition HOT-lift command/tooling blocker; and the source-backed payload/value `tablify(...)` boundary plus public stack-style one-target value-switch representation audit, showing that payload equality ladders are not a Binaryen `version_130` tablify target and public WAT stack-value switches already lift to child-form payloads before RUB-P collapse; and the note `1379` stack-representation boundary audit, adding focused coverage that public value `br_table` payloads lift as payload+selector children and public stack-form unreachable-input `br_on_cast` stays conservative until a raw proof or child-form HOT exposure exists; note `1380` remaining-GC boundary closure, narrowing descriptor BrOn, fallthrough/local.tee cast insertion, fallthrough-producing payload splits, public stack-form unreachable input, and nullable disjoint `SuccessOnlyIfNull` to exact blockers/non-goals with reopening criteria; and note `1381` large-table JumpThreader boundary coverage, adding a ten-target `br_table` guard test and keeping the `<= 9` cap until a precise predicate separates pure shell retargeting from mostly-default switch lowering; note `1382` final-optimizer boundary closeout, adding constant self-target `br_if` value coverage and retiring adjacent/value wording to exact source-backed boundaries; and note `1383` accepted-boundary consolidation for old-`try`, child-less stack-payload switches, unreachable-condition selectify, large-table JumpThreader, remaining GC, and final adjacent/value residue.     - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-S]` Eliminate raw-size regressions in the accepted dead-shell family. Completed 2026-06-29 with a split outcome. Implemented safe raw-size cleanup for the actionable owner from note `1394`: residual public stack-form `v128` same-target `br_table` payloads now collapse through the existing one-target value-switch path by treating exact `v128.const` SIMD payloads as reorder-safe. The reduced `.tmp/rub-s-v128-brtable.wat` improved from Starshine `80` vs Binaryen `65` raw bytes to Starshine `61` vs Binaryen `65`, and saved-lane reruns have `0` residual Starshine `br_table`: `1000-max100` improved from `+951` total raw delta to `-3353`, and runtime `max20` from `+428` to `-880`. The residual no-`br_table` positives are now narrowed to an intentional safety blocker rather than an avoidable RUB transform: the leading current case `case-000073-gen-valid` (`+38`) reduces to a void structured block with `return` in a result-typed function followed by a dead result suffix. `remove_unused_brs_root_suffix_required_after_void_structured_terminal(...)` must keep that suffix to avoid the stale result-suffix validation failure covered by existing tests, and the new boundary test `remove-unused-brs boundary keeps result suffix after void return block` locks the reduced owner. Reopen RUB-S only if a proof lands that can prune void-structured return suffixes in result-typed functions without invalidating HOT lowering, or if a new residual positive raw-size class is outside the v128 fixed owner and the guarded result-suffix family. Evidence: notes `1394` and `1395`; focused RUB tests pass `219/219`.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-T]` Fallthrough-producing GC payload split cleanup with localization proof. Completed 2026-06-29 as a precise localizer blocker rather than an implementation. Note `1373` already reduced the Binaryen `SuccessOnlyIfNonNull` payload split to the exact missing facility: Binaryen uses `ChildLocalizer`/scratch locals to move the branch payload and ref into an inner `br_on_non_null` while still dropping the payload on the not-taken fallthrough path, and a naive stack rewrite is invalid (`expected i32 but nothing on stack`). Starshine's current HOT/raw representation still lacks a RUB-local child localizer or scratch-local materialization proof for this shape, and the focused boundary test `remove-unused-brs boundary keeps fallthrough-producing payload br_on_cast split` keeps it fail-closed. Reopen only when RUB has a localizer-backed rewrite proving branch arity, fallthrough result type, single evaluation/order of payload and ref, correct payload dropping on fallthrough, and valid lowering without consuming values below an inner block's stack height. Evidence: notes `1373`, `1380`, and `1395`; focused RUB tests pass `219/219`.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-U]` Add descriptor `br_on_cast_desc_eq*` cleanup when representation support exists. Completed 2026-06-29 as a precise representation blocker. Current source still has leaf descriptor casts (`RefCastDescEq` / `HotOp::RefCastDescEq`) but no descriptor branch forms: repo search finds no `BrOnCastDesc*` or `br_on_cast_desc_eq*` branch instruction, and the only represented branch casts remain `BrOnCast` / `BrOnCastFail` in lib/HOT/lower/validate surfaces. RUB must not fake descriptor-branch cleanup in pass-only code until `Instruction`, binary decode/encode, WAT/WAST lowering, validator/typechecker, HOT lift/lower, and focused round-trip tests can carry both ref and descriptor operands. Reopen when descriptor BrOn representation exists end-to-end, then add red-first RUB descriptor cleanup tests against `remove-unused-brs-desc.wast`-style cases. Evidence: notes `1374`, `1380`, and `1396`; current source grep found leaf `RefCastDescEq` support but no branch descriptor op.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-V]` Extend large `br_table` JumpThreader safely beyond the nine-target cap. Completed 2026-06-29 for the safe pure-shell subset. Added a red-first positive `remove-unused-brs retargets pure ten-target br_table shell` that failed under the old `<= 9` cap, kept a new fail-closed `remove-unused-brs boundary keeps ten-target mostly-default br_table conservative`, and implemented `remove_unused_brs_branch_table_has_mostly_default_switch_shape(...)` so no-payload selector-only tables beyond nine targets retarget only when they are not the mostly-default switch-lowering shape that prior broad `32`-target experiments regressed. The predicate trims trailing defaults and leading defaults like `optimizeSwitch(...)`, then blocks all-first/last-nondefault-with-middle-default shapes; existing default-only/two-option/switch cleanup keeps owning those cases. Reopen for broader large-table retargeting only with tests proving mostly-default switch expectations, below-threshold mostly-default boundaries, and artifact raw gates stay green. Evidence: note `1396`; focused RUB test passed `220/220` after the red failure.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-W]` Expose public stack-form `br_on_cast*` unreachable-input cleanup. Completed 2026-06-29 as a narrowed candidate-lowering/raw-proof blocker. The implemented safe cleanup remains child-form HOT ordinary `br_on_cast*` whose ref child is explicit `unreachable`; public stack-form `unreachable; br_on_cast` still does not expose that child-form BrOn root, and a raw rewrite would need to prove stack effects, branch arity, dropped payload order/effects, validation, and refinalization before replacing the branch with dropped payloads plus `unreachable`. Existing boundary test `remove-unused-brs boundary keeps public stack-form unreachable-input br_on_cast` remains the correct fail-closed owner. Reopen only when HOT lift exposes public stack-form unreachable inputs as child-form BrOn roots or a raw pre-lift rewrite proof lands with validation evidence. Evidence: notes `1374`, `1379`, `1380`, and `1396`; focused RUB test passed `220/220`.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-RUB-X]` Evaluate branch-hint metadata and `remove-unused-brs-never-unconditionalize` parity as optimization-policy surfaces. Completed 2026-06-29 as a precise product-level representation/pass-option blocker rather than a pass implementation. RUB-N remains current after source recheck: Binaryen `branch-hints.h` and the branch-hints / branch-hints-shrink / branch-hints-unconditionalize lit files prove metadata movement and the `remove-unused-brs-never-unconditionalize` knob are real upstream contract surfaces, but Starshine still has only function/import `FuncAnnotationSec` metadata, no expression-level branch-hint/code-metadata representation, no parser/lowerer/binary round-trip for instruction-local `metadata.code.branch_hint`, no RUB remap target for `BranchHints::{copyTo, copyFlippedTo, applyAndTo, applyOrTo, flip, clear}`, and no public `--pass-arg` or pass-specific option carrier for `remove-unused-brs-never-unconditionalize`. Do not implement a superficial RUB-only hint rewrite or hidden flag until those project surfaces exist. Reopen with an expression-level code-metadata or opaque code-metadata preservation design, parser/printer/lowerer/binary round-trip tests for `if`/`br_if` hints, RUB hint-remap tests for each Binaryen helper behavior, and CLI/config/pass-manager tests for the never-unconditionalize option. Evidence: note `1397`.

  - Current Starshine coverage: exact `i32.add` / `i64.add` / `i32.sub` / `i64.sub` constant folding, direct integer `eq` / `ne` and unsigned relational constant compare folding with signed relational   - Coordination: keep descriptor `requires` truthfulness under `[AUDIT001-A]` / `[AUDIT001-B]`, but do not close OI on descriptor metadata alone. Coordinate raw skip threshold tests with `[AUDIT002-*]` where useful, while keeping OI-specific gates accountable here. New OI parity work should start from the machine-readable family matrix `docs/wiki/binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/parity-matrix.json` and grouped runner `scripts/oi-parity-sweep.ts`; update matrix rows/evidence instead of adding another one-behavior probe loop. The current `pass-oi-ref-gc` trigger smoke is deliberately non-descriptor after `.tmp/oi-ref-gc-grouped-sweep-20260627` showed the previous descriptor-bearing broad profile produced only wasm-tools `malformed section id: 14` generator failures in grouped OI-I/OI-J sweeps; `pass-oi-descriptor-gc` now provides a focused wasm-tools-valid descriptor smoke for OI-J, including pure and mutable-global effect-preserving known-null descriptor-cast labels. The first count1 timeout was isolated to `moon run --target native --release src/fuzz` compiling the native fuzz generator before case comparison; after prebuilding `src/fuzz`, descriptor lanes through `.tmp/oi-j-refgetdesc-nullable-child-tnh-count12-20260701`, `.tmp/oi-j-effectful-trapping-desc-block-count12-post-20260701`, `.tmp/oi-j-asnonnull-desc-cast-count12-20260701`, and `.tmp/oi-j-asnonnull-desc-cast-tnh-count12-20260701` normalized 12/12 with zero failures. The compare-pass parser now accepts `--traps-never-happen` and `--ignore-implicit-traps` as optimizer mode flags forwarded to both Starshine and Binaryen; `.tmp/oi-j-descriptor-tnh-compare-count12-20260701` and `.tmp/oi-j-descriptor-iit-compare-count12-20260701` each compared 12/12 descriptor-profile cases with 12 normalized matches and zero validation/generator/property/command failures. This is narrow constructor/getter/cast-eq/null-cast plus focused exact/strict-subtype/exact-subtype-miss and nullable exact-target null-only `ref.test` behavior only; exactness/useful-type-info breadth beyond the covered exact operands, exact-subtype exact-target misses, inexact strict-subtype exact-target misses, nullable exact-target null-only tests/casts, and exact same-target descriptor subsets, `ref.test_desc` binary-tool support, broader TNH/IIT beyond the current local-write `ref.as_non_null`, same-local nullable `ref.cast` equality IIT/TNH slices, standalone direct-local, branch-free block-child, and self-contained branch/branch-cast `ref.get_desc` TNH slices, plus focused descriptor-profile lanes, broader effectful/trapping descriptor operands beyond the flat effect-before-trap block-local slice, and broader descriptor behavior beyond the covered outer `ref.as_non_null` success-path fold remain open before OI-J can be classified as full parity. The first OI metamorphic smoke transforms, `oi-local-carried`, `oi-local-tee-wrapped`, `oi-commuted-operands`, `oi-memory-commuted-operands`, `oi-if-select-shell`, `oi-effectful-sibling`, `oi-trapping-sibling`, `oi-call-ref-target-wrapper`, `oi-memory-size-boundary`, and `oi-tuple-selected-lane`, now exist in `src/fuzz/metamorphic.mbt` and are forwarded by `scripts/oi-parity-sweep.ts`. The OI-D/OI-E/OI-G/OI-I/OI-M seed-indexed profiles now also write `profile_case_label` metadata into GenValid manifests, compare-pass `genValidProfileCaseCounts`, summary strategy counters, and `cases.jsonl`, and `scripts/oi-parity-sweep.ts` now prints per-row result summaries plus supports `--summarize-existing`, so raw mismatches can be grouped mechanically before opening WAT diffs. The first `.tmp/oi-parity-sweep-summary-smoke` representatives are classified in the matrix `classifiedCaseLabels`; the literal double-`eqz` missed-fixpoint candidate from OI-D is now implemented and `.tmp/oi-parity-sweep-oi-d-double-eqz-fix-smoke` narrows the remaining OI-D mismatches to sampled, measured-smaller constant compare/sign-extension extra-fold candidates (-5/-2/-2 canonical wasm bytes in the count-3 representatives). OI-E local-fact sign-extension cleanup is now a measured sampled Starshine-win candidate in `.tmp/oi-parity-sweep-summary-smoke`: all 12 raw/canonical artifacts validate, local-carried/direct sign-extension cases each remove two redundant sign-extension opcodes with canonical/WAT deltas `-2/-49` and raw bytes tied, and the local.tee/commuted case removes the same sign extensions plus folds a pure constant compare with raw/canonical/WAT deltas `-5/-5/-85`; the commuted case remains mixed with OI-D scalar drift and this is not broad LocalScanner closure. A fresh grouped OI-I/OI-J run at `.tmp/oi-ref-gc-trigger-grouped-sweep-20260627` now compares 3/3 per row with no generator/validation/property/command failures: OI-I local-carried null/test/cast matches, branch-cast boundaries are a size-losing result-type/tail-collapse mismatch (Starshine 108/96 raw/canonical bytes vs Binaryen 89/85), and the direct effectful case is mixed with OI-F/OI-D private-global/scalar spelling drift; the disabled OI-J row repeats the non-descriptor branch-cast mismatch and has a sampled Starshine-win dead trapping-sibling candidate (54/43 vs 74/74 bytes), but this is not descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT coverage. The seed-0x5eed `pass-oi-call-ref` stringref/exn wasm-tools validity blocker is fixed and now reaches a 1/1 compare with one raw mismatch originally classified as a large size-losing OI-H directization parity gap; follow-up raw-skip helpers now directize adjacent/block/constant-select/single-use-local ref.func call_ref wrappers and trim guaranteed null-as-non-null, dropped-unreachable-block, dropped null ref.test/ref.cast, and dropped non-null ref.cast tails before skipping, improving the target-wrapper smoke from 40 call_ref / 25 ref.func / >12KB Starshine raw output to 0 call_ref / 0 table.get / 20 direct call $5 / 572 raw bytes; a grouped count-3 OI-H sweep at `.tmp/oi-h-target-wrapper-grouped-sweep-20260627` now shows all sampled `oi-call-ref-target-wrapper`, `oi-local-carried`, and `oi-effectful-sibling` outputs have 0 call_ref/return_call_ref/table.get on both sides; a 2026-06-30 measurement validates all 12 grouped raw/canonical artifacts and classifies the residual mismatches as local-section/dropped-ref.func, local-section/dropped-null, and effectful-if/scalar cross-family shape drift rather than sampled OI-H target spelling, with aggregate Starshine raw/canonical/WAT deltas `+129/-72/-3473` (raw-size-losing despite smaller canonical/WAT); `oi-tuple-selected-lane` is only synthetic multivalue selected-lane smoke coverage; the current `pass-oi-tuple` smoke reaches count60 comparison with all-result, local-carried, helper-call-produced, local.tee-produced, select-produced, selected-trapping, selected-effectful, effectful-select-operand, trapping-select-operand, multi-selected-effectful, randomized-existing-effect-trap, runtime-selected-lane, direct, effectful-sibling, and trapping-sibling selected lanes, and the helper-call-produced, local.tee-produced, select-produced, selected-trapping, effectful-sibling, trapping-sibling, selected-effectful, effectful-select-operand, trapping-select-operand, plus multi-selected-effectful and randomized-existing-effect-trap residuals are classified as sampled Starshine-win candidates (helper-call Starshine/Binaryen raw 134/157, canonical 157/163; local.tee representative raw/canonical 86/107 and 107/112; select representative raw/canonical 106/127 and 127/132; selected-trapping representative raw/canonical 86/107 and 107/112; selected-effectful representative raw/canonical 377/569 and 569/670; effectful-select-operand direct representative raw/canonical 103/124 and 124/129, grouped representative 205/226 and 226/232; trapping-select-operand representative raw/canonical 144/166 and 166/171; multi-selected-effectful representative raw/canonical 179/226 and 226/243; randomized-existing-effect-trap representative raw/canonical 385/577 and 577/678; runtime-selected direct representative raw/canonical 83/106 and 106/111 with Node runtime equality; effectful-sibling representatives raw/canonical 196/219 and 219/224; trapping-sibling representative raw/canonical 136/159 and 159/165, with all raw/canonical outputs validating) rather than broad existing-producer/effect/trap/runtime tuple-scratch closure, so next OI generator work should use the existing OI-D/OI-E/OI-I `classifiedCaseLabels`, broaden and source-back the residual OI-D constant compare/sign-extension Starshine-win candidates beyond the count-3 sample, scale those seed-indexed trigger smokes into randomized constructors/transforms, split a true OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT profile from the non-descriptor OI-I ref smoke, continue reducing the OI-F `oi-effectful-sibling` grouped mismatch after the 2026-06-27 fix4 sweep at `.tmp/oi-f-effectful-family-fix4-20260627` (same-global `if` sinking and sampled dropped/nested `i32.add`, `i32.sub`, `i64.add`/`i64.sub`, and `i32.wrap_i64` constant folds now match; Bias toward deleting/condensing backlog uncertainty, assigning release states, and identifying boundaries; new generator breadth is a last resort. Current row states in `docs/wiki/binaryen/passes/optimize-instructions/parity-matrix.json`: OI-D=P0 `sampled-starshine-win` with a finite scalar closeout table and one exact v0.1 signoff command; OI-E=P0 `sampled-starshine-win` with a finite LocalScanner table, two resolved sampled implementation gaps, one exact v0.1 signoff command, and an explicit post-v0.1 broad LocalScanner tail; OI-G=P0 `accepted-release-boundary` based on zero runtime semantic mismatches where executable, validated residual artifacts, measured opcode/size classifications, and explicit memory64/shared/concurrency limits; OI-M=P0 `accepted-release-boundary` candidate based on validated runtime tuple lanes and raw/canonical Starshine wins with broader tuple-scratch reconstruction deferred; OI-F=P1 `blocked-surface` for branch-hint/code-metadata and never-fold/no-reorder option plumbing after sampled source-backed boolean/select residuals normalized and now has a finite v0.1 boundary/signoff action; OI-H=P1 `defer-with-reopen-criteria` for broader table-state/return_call_ref/multivalue/effect target directization; OI-I=P1 `accepted-release-boundary` for v0.1 non-descriptor ref basics with finite signoff/reopen criteria; OI-J=P1 `blocked-surface` until true descriptor-compatible generator/oracle/toolchain evidence exists; OI-K=P2 `accepted-release-boundary` for represented pure/default GC constructor subsets and trap-preserving limits; OI-L=P2 `implemented-final-probe-smoke` for the current sampled aggregate atomic struct-rewrite/array-preserve surface; reopen only for changed binary/proposal semantics, a dedicated profile mismatch, new Binaryen array rewrites, or true shared/concurrent runtime evidence. Next work should prioritize P1/P2 closeout boundary language or finite signoff decisions; OI-D/OI-E should only run their finite signoff commands unless a concrete gap appears, OI-F should only run its finite count40 non-metadata smoke if release policy requires it, and OI-G/OI-M should remain untouched unless a reopen criterion fires.

Timeout triage showed the apparent count1 hang was native `src/fuzz` generator compilation inside `moon run`, not Binaryen, Starshine cmd, descriptor decoding, cache, or per-case process handling; after prebuilding `src/fuzz`, `.tmp/oi-j-descriptor-gc-profile-count1-debug2-20260701` compared 1/1 with 1 normalized match and zero failures. This slice also reduced the HOT representation blocker: red-first `src/ir/hot_lift_test.mbt::hot lift preserves official two-operand descriptor cast operands` failed with a unary child count, then passed after `RefCastDescEq` stopped forcing a unary HOT pop count; `src/ir/hot_lower_test.mbt::hot lower roundtrips official two-operand descriptor cast` and `.tmp/oi-j-descriptor-hot-two-operand-count8-20260701` (8/8 normalized, zero failures) passed. This narrows the old generator/tooling blocker from `.tmp/oi-ref-gc-grouped-sweep-20260627` (`malformed section id 14`) but does not close OI-J.       - Additional coverage 2026-06-24: same-local integer binary operand folding now rewrites direct identical `local.get` operands for `sub` and `xor` to zero, and rewrites same-local `and` / `or` to the local value for i32 and i64. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-same-local-binary-probe.wat`; red-first focused `*same-local integer binary operands*` failed before implementation and passed after. The proof is deliberately direct-local only; broader expression identity remains open.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-24: first pure i32 masked unsigned compare fold. Starshine now folds `eq`/`ne` and unsigned relational compares when a side-effect-free `i32.and` with a nonnegative constant mask proves the value range is below the compared constant, e.g. `(x & 255) == 256 -> 0`, `(x & 255) <_u 256 -> 1`, and `(x & 255) >=_u -1 -> 0`. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probes `.tmp/oi-d-mask-compare-probe.wat` and `.tmp/oi-d-mask-compare-probe2.wat`; red-first focused `*i32 masked unsigned compares*` failed before implementation and passed after.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-24: the same pure direct mask proof now covers i64 `and` values for `eq`/`ne` and unsigned relational compares, e.g. `(x & 255L) == 256L -> 0`, `(x & 255L) <_u 256L -> 1`, and `(x & 255L) >=_u -1L -> 0`. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-i64-mask-compare-probe.wat`; red-first focused `*i64 masked unsigned compares*` failed before implementation and passed after. The effect-preserving i64 variant is covered below; recursive maxBits proofs beyond direct nonnegative `and` masks, signed range proofs, and non-mask width facts remain open.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-24: effect-preserving i32 masked unsigned compare folds now match Binaryen's direct `drop(masked-value); i32.const` shape for direct `i32.and` operands with a nonnegative constant mask and an effectful/trapping non-mask operand, e.g. `(call $effect & 255) == 256 -> drop(i32.and(call $effect, 255)); i32.const 0`. Evidence: `.tmp/oi-d-mask-compare-probe.wat` shows Binaryen preserving the masked call operand; red-first focused `*i32 masked unsigned compare effects*` failed before implementation and passed after. Recursive maxBits facts beyond direct nonnegative `and` masks, signed range proofs, and non-mask width facts remain open.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-24: effect-preserving i64 masked unsigned compare folds now match Binaryen's direct `drop(masked-value); i32.const` shape for direct `i64.and` operands with a nonnegative constant mask and an effectful/trapping non-mask operand, e.g. `(call $effect & 255L) == 256L -> drop(i64.and(call $effect, 255L)); i32.const 0`. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-i64-mask-compare-effects-probe.wat`; red-first focused `*i64 masked unsigned compare effects*` failed before implementation and passed after. Recursive maxBits facts beyond direct nonnegative `and` masks, signed range proofs, and non-mask width facts remain open.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-24: first recursive `maxBits`-style i32 unsigned-shift bounded compare fold. Starshine now folds `eq`/`ne` and unsigned relational compares when a direct `i32.shr_u` with constant shift amount `1..31` proves the value range is below the compared constant, preserving effectful/trapping shifted operands as `drop(i32.shr_u(...)); i32.const result`; e.g. `(call $effect >>> 8) == 16777216 -> drop(i32.shr_u(call $effect, 8)); i32.const 0`. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-shr-maxbits-probe.wat`; red-first focused `*i32 unsigned-shift bounded compares*` failed before implementation and passed after. The proof remains constant-shift-only; i64 shift facts are covered below, while dynamic/zero shift amounts, signed range proofs, and broader recursive `maxBits` facts remain open.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-24: the same recursive `maxBits`-style unsigned-shift bounded compare fold now covers direct `i64.shr_u` with constant shift amount `1..63`, preserving effectful/trapping shifted operands as `drop(i64.shr_u(...)); i32.const result`; e.g. `(call $effect >>> 8) == 72057594037927936 -> drop(i64.shr_u(call $effect, 8)); i32.const 0`. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-i64-shr-maxbits-probe.wat`; red-first focused `*i64 unsigned-shift bounded compares*` failed before implementation and passed after. Dynamic/zero shift amounts, signed range proofs, and broader recursive `maxBits` facts remain open.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-24: direct recursive i32/i64 `maxBits` facts now flow through nested direct `and`/`shr_u` children, so shapes like `((x & 1023) >>> 4) == 64` fold to `i32.const 0` and effectful nested i32 operands become `drop(i32.shr_u(i32.and(call, 1023), 4)); i32.const 0`, matching Binaryen's observed `version_130` shape. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-recursive-maxbits-probe.wat`; red-first focused `*recursive unsigned maxBits compares*` failed before implementation and passed after. Dynamic/zero shifts, signed range proofs, local-scanner facts, select/phi/extension facts, and broader recursive expressions remain open.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-25: first direct i32 sign-extension equality range fold. Starshine now folds `i32.eq` / `i32.ne` when a direct `i32.extend8_s` or `i32.extend16_s` result is compared with a constant outside the signed lane range, preserving effectful inputs with `drop(input); i32.const result`; i64 sign-extension equality and signed relational range folds remain open because the observed Binaryen `version_130` probe kept those forms. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-signext-compare-probe.wat`; red-first focused `*sign-extension equality*` failed before implementation and passed after.
      - Additional boundary coverage 2026-06-25: `docs/wiki/raw/research/0878-2026-06-25-optimize-instructions-oi-d-i64-signext-equality-boundary.md` locks the i64 sign-extension equality boundary. Binaryen `version_130` keeps out-of-range `i64.extend8_s` / `i64.extend16_s` / `i64.extend32_s` equality or inequality comparisons, and Starshine now has public-pipeline coverage proving it keeps those forms instead of generalizing the covered i32 range fold. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-i64-signext-equality-probe.wat`; focused `*i64 sign-extension equality*` passed `1/1`.
      - Additional boundary coverage 2026-06-25: `docs/wiki/raw/research/0889-2026-06-25-optimize-instructions-oi-d-i32-signext-rel-boundary.md` locks direct i32 sign-extension signed-relational out-of-range comparisons as keep-spelling. Binaryen `version_130` keeps `i32.lt_s(i32.extend8_s(...), 128)` and `i32.gt_s(i32.extend16_s(...), -32769)`, and Starshine now has public-pipeline coverage proving it does not generalize the covered equality-only fold to those relational forms. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-i32-signext-rel-boundary-probe.wat`; focused `*i32 sign-extension relational*` passed `1/1`.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-25: `docs/wiki/raw/research/0896-2026-06-25-optimize-instructions-oi-d-load-maxbits.md` extends the narrow unsigned `maxBits` compare proof to direct unsigned load result widths. Starshine now folds out-of-range compares over `i32.load8_u` / `i32.load16_u` and analogous i64 unsigned loads while preserving the trapping load as `drop(load); i32.const result`, matching Binaryen's observed `version_130` shape. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-load8u-maxbits-probe.wat`; red-first focused `*unsigned load maxBits*` failed before implementation and passed after.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-25: `docs/wiki/raw/research/0898-2026-06-25-optimize-instructions-oi-d-maxbits-signed-rel.md` extends the same direct nonnegative `maxBits` proof to signed relational out-of-range comparisons. Starshine now folds pure `i32.gt_s((x & 255), 300)`-style shapes and effect-preserving unsigned-load signed comparisons such as `i32.le_s(i32.load8_u(ptr), 300)` while preserving effectful/trapping operands. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-maxbits-signed-rel-probe.wat`; red-first focused `*unsigned maxBits signed compares*` failed before implementation and passed after.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-26: `docs/wiki/raw/research/1323-2026-06-26-optimize-instructions-oi-d-double-eqz.md` adds red-first double-`eqz` cleanup. Starshine now rewrites direct `i32.eqz(i32.eqz(x))` and `i32.eqz(i64.eqz(x))` to `i32.ne(x, 0)` / `i64.ne(x, 0)`, preserving the original operand and matching Binaryen `version_130` oracle evidence. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-eqz-eqz-probe.wat`; focused `*double eqz*` failed before implementation and passed after.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-26: `docs/wiki/raw/research/1325-2026-06-26-optimize-instructions-oi-d-zero-compare-eqz.md` adds red-first zero-compare `eqz` inversion. Starshine now rewrites direct `i32.eqz(i32.ne(x, 0))` / `i32.eqz(i64.ne(x, 0))` to `i32.eqz(x)` / `i64.eqz(x)` and `i32.eqz(i32.eq(x, 0))` / `i32.eqz(i64.eq(x, 0))` to `i32.ne(x, 0)` / `i64.ne(x, 0)`, preserving the nonzero operand and matching Binaryen `version_130` oracle evidence. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-probe.wat`; focused `*zero compare*` failed before implementation and passed after.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-26: `docs/wiki/raw/research/1327-2026-06-26-optimize-instructions-oi-d-signed-domain-edge.md` adds red-first signed domain-edge compare folding. Starshine now folds side-effect-free `x <s INT_MIN`, `x >=s INT_MIN`, `x >s INT_MAX`, and `x <=s INT_MAX` i32/i64 compares to constants, while keeping call-backed signed endpoint compares because the Binaryen oracle kept that effectful shape. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-signed-domain-probe.wat`; focused `*signed domain-edge*` failed before implementation and passed after.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-27: `docs/wiki/raw/research/1329-2026-06-27-optimize-instructions-oi-d-eqz-relational-compare.md` adds red-first integer-relational `eqz(compare)` inversion. Starshine now rewrites i32/i64 signed and unsigned `lt`/`le`/`gt`/`ge` compares under `i32.eqz` to the inverted relational opcode while preserving operand order, matching Binaryen `version_130` local-backed evidence. Float relational compares and nonzero integer eq/ne inversion remain outside this narrow slice. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-compare-eqz-rel-probe.wat`; focused `*integer compares into inverted compares*` failed before implementation and passed after; focused zero-compare guard passed after implementation.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-27: `docs/wiki/raw/research/1331-2026-06-27-optimize-instructions-oi-d-eqz-float-eqne.md` adds red-first float equality/inequality `eqz(compare)` inversion. Starshine now rewrites `i32.eqz(f32.eq/ne(...))` and `i32.eqz(f64.eq/ne(...))` to the opposite float equality opcode while preserving operands, matching Binaryen `version_130` oracle evidence. Float relational `eqz(compare)` remains a boundary because Binaryen kept the probed `i32.eqz(f32.lt(...))` shape.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-25: `docs/wiki/raw/research/0900-2026-06-25-optimize-instructions-oi-d-maxbits-signed-spelling.md` adds in-range signed-to-unsigned spelling canonicalization for the same direct nonnegative `maxBits` proof surface. Starshine now rewrites `i32.lt_s((x & 255), 128)` to `i32.lt_u`, `i64.ge_s((x >>> 8), 42)` to `i64.ge_u`, and unsigned-load signed comparisons such as `i32.lt_s(i32.load8_u(ptr), 128)` to unsigned spellings while preserving the trapping load. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-maxbits-signed-spelling-probe.wat`; red-first focused `*unsigned maxBits signed compare spelling*` failed before implementation and passed after.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-25: `docs/wiki/raw/research/0902-2026-06-25-optimize-instructions-oi-d-local-maxbits.md` adds the first straight-line local-carried unsigned max facts for compare folds and signed-to-unsigned spelling rewrites. Starshine now carries covered direct `and`, positive constant `shr_u`, unsigned-load, and nested max facts through direct `local.set` / `local.tee` roots to later `local.get` compares, while structured control invalidates conservatively. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-maxbits-local-probe.wat`; focused `*local unsigned maxBits compares*` exposed the local-carried behavior gap during development and passed after implementation. Full `LocalScanner` parity, CFG/phi/select facts, dynamic/zero shifts, and broader producer families remain open.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-25: `docs/wiki/raw/research/0904-2026-06-25-optimize-instructions-oi-d-wrap-maxbits.md` adds the first `i32.wrap_i64` unsigned max fact producer. Starshine now inherits an i32 max fact from wrapped i64 producers only when the proven i64 maximum is nonnegative and no larger than `i32::MAX`, enabling direct out-of-range folds and local-carried signed-to-unsigned spelling rewrites. Evidence: Binaryen oracle probe `.tmp/oi-d-wrap-maxbits-probe.wat`; focused `*i32.wrap_i64 unsigned maxBits*` failed before implementation and passed `1/1` after. Wider i64 wrap ranges and broader `LocalScanner` behavior remain open.

      - Additional boundary coverage 2026-06-25: `docs/wiki/raw/research/0908-2026-06-25-optimize-instructions-oi-d-i64-dynamic-shift-maxbits-boundary.md` locks the i64 sibling dynamic-shift maxBits boundary.       - Additional coverage 2026-06-25: `docs/wiki/raw/research/1150-2026-06-25-optimize-instructions-oi-d-shift-rotate-mask-coverage.md` locks already-implemented constant shift/rotate effective-amount masking. Binaryen `version_130` reduces `i32.shl(x, 32)`, `i64.shl(x, 64)`, `i32.rotl(x, 32)`, and `i64.rotl(x, 64)` to `x`, and canonicalizes nonzero masked amounts such as `i32.shr_u(x, 63)`, `i32.shr_s(x, -1)`, `i64.shr_u(x, 127)`, `i32.rotr(x, 63)`, and `i64.rotr(x, 127)` to `31`/`63`; Starshine already matched through existing constant-fold/identity machinery, so this is coverage/status evidence rather than red-first implementation work.
      - Additional coverage 2026-06-26: `docs/wiki/raw/research/1154-2026-06-26-optimize-instructions-oi-d-signext-unsigned-top.md` adds a red-first i32 sign-extension unsigned-top compare rewrite.       - Additional coverage 2026-06-26: `docs/wiki/raw/research/1156-2026-06-26-optimize-instructions-oi-d-i64-signext-unsigned-top.md` adds the i64 sibling for sign-extension unsigned-top compares.     - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-E]` Add `LocalScanner`-style width/sign facts and first sign-extension slice. Completed 2026-06-19 in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0730-2026-06-19-optimize-instructions-oi-e-sign-ext-facts.md`: added a conservative HOT-local sign-extension fact scan with param-pessimistic and non-param-default facts, straight-line `local.set` / `local.tee` fallthrough updates, signed-load and explicit sign-extension recognition, redundant sign-extension removal, and `shl` + `shr_s` sign-extension idiom synthesis. Focused coverage locks signed-load removal, local-set facts, param pessimism, loop-carried/control invalidation, and shift-idiom rewriting. Evidence: focused sign filters passed `12/12`, full focused optimize-instructions filter passed `98/98`, `moon fmt` passed, `moon test src/passes` passed `2610/2610`, native `src/cmd` release build passed with existing unused-function warnings, and direct `--pass optimize-instructions --count 10000 --seed 0x5eed --jobs auto --starshine-bin target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe` in `.tmp/pass-fuzz-optimize-instructions-oi-e-sign-ext-safe-10000` compared `53/10000` before the default failure ceiling with `26` normalized matches, `27` raw mismatches agent-classified as Starshine-win constant-if and redundant-sign-extension-removal shape differences, `0` validation/property/generator failures, and `1` known Binaryen/tool command failure (`binaryen-rec-group-zero`). Full Binaryen `maxBits` mask/compare proofs and CFG joins remain open for later slices.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-OI-F]` Classify and widen boolean/select/ternary shell parity. Completed 2026-06-19 in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0731-2026-06-19-optimize-instructions-oi-f-boolean-select-shells.md`: classified existing `if` condition inversion, nested boolean-tree cleanup, boolean value-if wrapping, duplicate-branch collapse, and constant-if Starshine-win behavior; added constant-condition `select` cleanup when the dropped arm is side-effect-free; kept a call-effect dropped-arm negative because WebAssembly evaluates both select values; and documented branch-hint/code-metadata plus `optimize-instructions-never-fold-or-reorder` as explicit unsupported metadata/options boundaries. Evidence: red-first select filter failed the two positive folds before implementation and kept the effect negative passing; final select filter passed `3/3`, full focused optimize-instructions filter passed `101/101`, `moon fmt` passed, `moon test src/passes` passed `2613/2613`, native `src/cmd` build passed with existing warnings, and direct `--pass optimize-instructions --count 10000 --seed 0x5eed --jobs auto --starshine-bin target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe` in `.tmp/pass-fuzz-optimize-instructions-oi-f-select-safe-10000` compared `54/10000` before the default failure ceiling with `27` normalized matches, `27` raw mismatches agent-classified as known Starshine-win constant-if and redundant-sign-extension output-shape differences, `0` validation/property/generator failures, and `1` known Binaryen/tool command failure (`binaryen-rec-group-zero`).
      - Grouped transform evidence 2026-06-27: `.tmp/oi-f-effectful-family-classify-20260627` ran the family-first OI-F count-4 sweep and grouped all four transform labels before one-off probes. It compared 4/4 with raw mismatches for `oi-if-select-shell`, `oi-effectful-sibling`, `oi-trapping-sibling`, and `oi-commuted-operands`, with no validation/generator/property/command failures and all raw/canonical outputs validating. The pure if/select shell, dead trapping sibling, and commuted-constant representatives are sampled canonical-size Starshine-win candidates under their synthetic transform contracts. Follow-up red-first raw-skip slices through `.tmp/oi-f-effectful-family-fix7-20260627` removed the sampled private-global `oi-effectful-sibling` normalized mismatch: the grouped sweep now reports `transform:oi-effectful-sibling` as a normalized match, with normalized Binaryen/Starshine size 2893/2893 and matching sampled integer/sign/float opcode counts after same-global if sinking, sampled integer constant folds, nonnegative signed-to-unsigned scalar spellings, finite float sub-to-add-negative rewrites, and the sampled f64 div-by-two/sub rewrite. Raw Starshine remains larger at 4135 vs Binaryen 2893 as a separate raw encoder/structure residual. A fix7 `--summarize-existing` refresh re-measured the remaining `oi-if-select-shell`, `oi-trapping-sibling`, and `oi-commuted-operands` labels and kept them classified as sampled canonical Starshine-win candidates (2732/2844, 2705/2845, and 2755/2855 Starshine/Binaryen canonical bytes respectively), not size-losing blockers; next OI-F grouped work should upgrade those synthetic labels to live trap/effect/existing-producer wrappers and branch-hint/no-fold metadata coverage.

- [O4Z-AUDIT-PC] - Deep audit `precompute`
  - Status: completed for v0.1.0 direct `precompute` audit closure on 2026-06-20 under the modern pass-audit standard. The 2026-06-06 branch-heavy direct compare blocker remains cleared, `[AUDIT001-E]` descriptor split coverage is complete, the dedicated `precompute-all` GenValid profile exists, the first O4z recovery slice re-enables only changed raw scalar folds under the O4z gate, the native compare path is explicitly `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe` in this checkout, the first regular-GenValid reduction fixed the constant self-exiting `block br_if` subfamily, the next reduction fixed constant-true self-branching loop result tails by rewriting them to `loop (br ...)` plus `unreachable`, and the remaining constant-false loop / mixed root-debris family has source-backed compare closeout handling through the `loop (nop)` extension to the PC `unreachable-control-debris` normalizer. The O4z no-op boundary is explicitly accepted for v0.1.0 with reopening criteria in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0793-2026-06-20-precompute-o4z-boundary-decision.md`. Final closeout in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0795-2026-06-20-precompute-final-closeout.md` accepts the explicit wasm-smith reachable-`atomic.fence` preservation divergence as a narrow Starshine correctness boundary and records the missing regular GenValid `100000` lane green.
  - Scope: constant folding, trap/effect preservation, raw precleaner/writeback guards, direct `precompute` versus private `precompute-propagate-prefix`, GC/array atomic exclusions, current O4z raw-scalar recovery plus the accepted/fail-closed `o4z-precompute-noop` boundary, and O4z slot19/slot43 history.
  - Current finding: `precompute-all` now exists as the dedicated composite GenValid profile, with singleton leaves for scalar folds, constant-if/control cleanup, immutable globals, raw drop/block cleanup, trap/effect boundaries, GC/array atomic boundaries, and direct-vs-prefix watchpoints; focused generator coverage plus `docs/wiki/raw/research/0787-2026-06-20-precompute-dedicated-genvalid-profile.md` prove resolution, aggregate sampling, validating modules, and selected leaf metadata. Source review in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0785-2026-06-20-precompute-modern-signoff-refresh.md` found `precompute_descriptor()` has no `requires`, `precompute_propagate_prefix_descriptor()` requires `ssa`, and direct source calls `pass_require_ssa(...)` only from the private prefix helper. The focused registry test `precompute descriptor split keeps direct pass SSA-free and prefix SSA-backed` plus `docs/wiki/raw/research/0786-2026-06-20-precompute-descriptor-split-audit.md` close `[AUDIT001-E]`; `[AUDIT001-F]` is not needed unless future source changes make direct `precompute` request SSA. `docs/wiki/raw/research/0788-2026-06-20-precompute-o4z-raw-scalar-recovery.md` reopens only the existing `raw-scalar-folds` subset under `optimize_level >= 4 && shrink_level >= 1`; HOT-only cleanup, load/call ownership hazards, large lowered functions, br_table/parser stack hazards, raw no-candidate cases, and non-scalar raw repair reasons still return `o4z-precompute-noop`. `docs/wiki/raw/research/0789-2026-06-20-precompute-native-path-and-bounded-evidence.md` resolves the local native path to `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe`, records a green `1000/1000` dedicated-profile smoke, and opens a regular GenValid behavior-parity mismatch family. `docs/wiki/raw/research/0790-2026-06-20-precompute-self-branch-reduction.md` fixes the first reduced self-exiting `block br_if` gap, `docs/wiki/raw/research/0791-2026-06-20-precompute-true-loop-tail-reduction.md` fixes the constant-true self-branching loop result-tail subfamily, and `docs/wiki/raw/research/0792-2026-06-20-precompute-loop-nop-closeout-normalizer.md` keeps the focused-test-backed constant-false self-branching loop cleanup as a size-winning Starshine output while extending the PC normalizer to erase exact `loop (nop)` / empty void wrapper debris. `docs/wiki/raw/research/0793-2026-06-20-precompute-o4z-boundary-decision.md` explicitly accepts the remaining O4z fail-closed surface for v0.1.0: only changed `raw-scalar-folds` run under O4z, while HOT-only cleanup, load/call ownership hazards, large lowered functions, br_table/parser stack hazards, unchanged raw no-candidate cases, and changed non-scalar repair reasons remain `o4z-precompute-noop`; `.artifacts` is absent in this checkout, so slot19/slot43 replay remains a historical/non-runnable evidence surface until fixtures return or are replaced.
  - Latest direct evidence: `.tmp/pass-fuzz-precompute-branch-heavy-slice4-norm4-10000` with `--normalize drop-consts --normalize local-cleanup-debris --normalize unreachable-control-debris` compared `6769/10000`, normalized `3375`, cleanup-normalized `3394`, mismatches `0`, command failures `20` (Binaryen/tool decode classes). Branch-heavy constant-control drift is covered by focused raw cleanup and semantic-safe normalizer tests. The 2026-06-20 profile smoke `.tmp/pass-fuzz-precompute-genvalid-precompute-all-smoke-50-norm2` used the same PC normalizers on `--gen-valid-profile precompute-all`, compared `50/50`, normalized `25`, cleanup-normalized `25`, mismatches/failures `0`, and sampled all seven leaves. The O4z recovery smoke `.tmp/pass-fuzz-precompute-genvalid-precompute-all-o4z-slice-smoke-100` used explicit native `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe` with `--jobs auto`, compared `100/100`, normalized `53`, cleanup-normalized `47`, mismatches/failures `0`, and sampled all seven leaves. The native-path slice raised the dedicated lane to `.tmp/pass-fuzz-precompute-native-path-policy-precompute-all-1000`, compared `1000/1000`, normalized `544`, cleanup-normalized `456`, mismatches/failures `0`, and sampled all seven leaves. Follow-up reductions fixed the self-exiting-block and true-loop-tail subfamilies, and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-precompute-loop-nop-normalizer-direct-100` with the updated PC normalizer compared `100/100`, normalized `16`, cleanup-normalized `84`, mismatches/failures `0`. The refresh in `0794` adds `.tmp/pass-fuzz-precompute-final-refresh-direct-10000` (`10000/10000`, normalized `1547`, cleanup-normalized `8453`, mismatches/failures `0`), `.tmp/pass-fuzz-precompute-final-refresh-precompute-all-10000` (`10000/10000`, normalized `5413`, cleanup-normalized `4587`, all seven leaves sampled, mismatches/failures `0`), and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-precompute-final-refresh-pass-fuzz-stress-10000` (`10000/10000`, normalized `1529`, cleanup-normalized `8471`, mismatches/failures `0`). The explicit wasm-smith lane `.tmp/pass-fuzz-precompute-final-refresh-wasm-smith-10000` compared `9956/10000` with `9952` normalized, `3` cleanup-normalized, `44` Binaryen/oracle command failures, and `1` mismatch: `case-006523-wasm-smith`, where Binaryen erases a reachable `atomic.fence` before branch-to-end and Starshine preserves it as an ordering barrier; `0795` accepts that as a narrow Starshine correctness boundary and adds `.tmp/pass-fuzz-precompute-final-regular-100000` (`100000/100000`, normalized `15491`, cleanup-normalized `84509`, mismatches/failures `0`, command failures `0`).

First 2026-06-20 widening slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0806-2026-06-20-code-pushing-unreachable-arm-post-use.md`: Starshine now matches Binaryen's narrow `optimizeIntoIf` post-use allowance when all in-arm reads are in one `if` arm, any additional reads are same-region suffix reads after the `if`, and the non-consuming arm cannot fall through under the conservative local proof. Second 2026-06-20 inventory slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/binaryen/2026-06-20-code-pushing-version-130-source-lit-refresh.md` and `docs/wiki/raw/research/0807-2026-06-20-code-pushing-version-130-source-lit-refresh.md`: the local oracle is `wasm-opt version 130`, the current lit surface is the named family set, movement checks now use `effects.orderedBefore(cumulativeEffects)`, and `code-pushing-atomics.wast` is an explicit remaining proof surface. Third 2026-06-20 inventory slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0808-2026-06-20-code-pushing-segment-inventory.md`: Starshine now has a non-mutating segment-window diagnostic for candidate `local.set` roots, selected push-point kinds, SFA rejection reasons, and a coarse ordered-before/effect barrier. Fourth 2026-06-20 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0809-2026-06-20-code-pushing-if-segment-movement.md`: Starshine now consumes `candidate:if` for a first single-set ordinary-void-`if` segment movement family, moving the set after the `if` when the `if` does not read the local and all reads are same-region suffix reads. Fifth 2026-06-20 profile slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0810-2026-06-20-code-pushing-dedicated-genvalid-profile.md`: `code-pushing-all` became the dedicated profile for implemented positive movement families and the checkout-local native path was refreshed to `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe`. Sixth 2026-06-20 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0811-2026-06-20-code-pushing-dropped-if-segment-movement.md`: Starshine now consumes `candidate:dropped-if` for the corresponding single-set dropped value-`if` movement family, and `code-pushing-all` includes a new `code-pushing-dropped-if` leaf. Seventh 2026-06-20 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0812-2026-06-20-code-pushing-br-if-segment-movement.md`: Starshine now consumes `candidate:conditional-branch` for a narrow no-branch-value `br_if` to a void block label, and `code-pushing-all` includes a new `code-pushing-br-if` leaf. Eighth 2026-06-20 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0813-2026-06-20-code-pushing-ordered-multi-set-movement.md`: Starshine now preserves source order when moving adjacent local-independent SFA sets after an ordinary void `if`, and `code-pushing-all` includes a new `code-pushing-multi-set` leaf. Ninth 2026-06-20 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0814-2026-06-20-code-pushing-dropped-if-multi-set-movement.md`: Starshine now preserves source order when moving adjacent local-independent SFA sets after a dropped value `if`, and `code-pushing-all` includes a new `code-pushing-multi-set-dropped-if` leaf. Tenth 2026-06-20 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0815-2026-06-20-code-pushing-br-if-multi-set-movement.md`: Starshine now preserves source order when moving adjacent local-independent SFA sets after a narrow no-branch-value `br_if` to a void block label, and `code-pushing-all` includes a new `code-pushing-multi-set-br-if` leaf. Eleventh 2026-06-20 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0816-2026-06-20-code-pushing-local-copy-multi-set-movement.md`: Starshine now preserves source order when moving adjacent direct local-copy SFA sets after ordinary void `if`, dropped value-`if`, and narrow `br_if` push points when source locals are stable, and `code-pushing-all` includes a new `code-pushing-multi-set-local-copy` leaf. Twelfth 2026-06-20 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0817-2026-06-20-code-pushing-nop-window-multi-set-movement.md`: Starshine now preserves source order for local-independent SFA multi-set windows separated only by `nop` roots before ordinary void `if`, dropped value-`if`, and narrow `br_if` push points, and `code-pushing-all` includes a new `code-pushing-multi-set-nop-window` leaf. Thirteenth 2026-06-20 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0818-2026-06-20-code-pushing-loop-br-if-movement.md`: Starshine now treats no-branch-value `br_if` to a void loop label as a supported push point for existing single-set, ordered multi-set, and direct local-copy movement, and `code-pushing-all` includes a new `code-pushing-loop-br-if` leaf. Fourteenth 2026-06-21 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0819-2026-06-21-code-pushing-drop-window-multi-set-movement.md`: Starshine now preserves source order for local-independent SFA multi-set windows separated by a dead `drop(const)` root before ordinary void `if`, dropped value-`if`, and narrow block-/loop-target `br_if` push points, and `code-pushing-all` includes a new `code-pushing-multi-set-drop-window` leaf. Fifteenth 2026-06-21 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0820-2026-06-21-code-pushing-local-get-window-multi-set-movement.md`: Starshine now preserves source order for local-independent SFA multi-set windows separated by `drop(local.get)` before ordinary void `if`, dropped value-`if`, and narrow block-/loop-target `br_if` push points. Sixteenth 2026-06-21 movement slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0821-2026-06-21-code-pushing-global-get-window-multi-set-movement.md`: Starshine now preserves source order for local-independent SFA multi-set windows separated by `drop(global.get)` before ordinary void `if` and dropped value-`if` push points, while keeping the same global-get separator window stationary before block-/loop-target `br_if` to match Binaryen v130 probes, and `code-pushing-all` includes a new `code-pushing-multi-set-global-get-window` leaf. Seventeenth 2026-06-21 switch-boundary slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0822-2026-06-21-code-pushing-br-table-boundary.md`: Binaryen v130 kept simple no-branch-value `br_table` windows stationary, so Starshine protects that no-mutation boundary. Eighteenth 2026-06-21 atomics/GC slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0823-2026-06-21-code-pushing-atomics-gc-boundary.md`: Starshine now admits the official `code-pushing-atomics.wast` non-null `struct.get` heap-read family across atomic loads for into-`if` and segment movement while preserving atomic stores as memory-write boundaries. Nineteenth/twentieth 2026-06-24 branch-value `br_if` slices completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0824-2026-06-24-code-pushing-branch-value-br-if.md` and `docs/wiki/raw/research/0825-2026-06-24-code-pushing-branch-value-multiset-br-if.md`: Starshine now handles single-set and adjacent ordered multi-set movement after value-block-target `br_if`, while the targeted leaf remains out of `code-pushing-all` pending the value-`br_if` lowering representation gap. Twenty-first 2026-06-24 `br_on_null` slice completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0826-2026-06-24-code-pushing-br-on-null-movement.md`: Starshine now handles single-set and adjacent ordered multi-set movement after dropped void-label `br_on_null`, and `code-pushing-all` includes the aggregate-safe `code-pushing-br-on-null` leaf. Twenty-second/twenty-third/twenty-fourth 2026-06-24 `br_on_non_null` / `br_on_cast` / `br_on_cast_fail` slices completed in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0827-2026-06-24-code-pushing-br-on-non-null-inventory.md`, `docs/wiki/raw/research/0828-2026-06-24-code-pushing-br-on-cast-movement.md`, and `docs/wiki/raw/research/0829-2026-06-24-code-pushing-br-on-cast-fail-movement.md`: Starshine now handles single-set and adjacent ordered multi-set movement after one-result-block `br_on_non_null`, dropped one-result-block `br_on_cast`, and dropped one-result-block `br_on_cast_fail`, and `code-pushing-all` includes aggregate-safe leaves for all three. The 2026-06-25 loop-label boundary probes in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0830-2026-06-25-code-pushing-br-on-non-null-loop-boundary.md`, `docs/wiki/raw/research/0831-2026-06-25-code-pushing-br-on-cast-loop-boundary.md`, and `docs/wiki/raw/research/0832-2026-06-25-code-pushing-br-on-cast-fail-loop-boundary.md` cover one-result loop-label `br_on_non_null`, `br_on_cast`, and `br_on_cast_fail` as narrow Binaryen-stationary boundaries.
