  - Closeout / reopen contract: `0892` closes the old active audit with current four-lane evidence (`0888` dedicated `code-pushing-all`, `0889` wasm-smith, `0890` regular GenValid, `0891` broad named `pass-fuzz-stress`) plus focused `121/121`, `src/passes` `2830/2830`, and full `moon test` `6160/6160`. Reopen under a new source-backed slice if a reduced Binaryen v130/current-main probe shows positive movement Starshine misses; a protected stationary boundary starts moving upstream; direct compare finds an unexplained raw mismatch beyond documented `local-cleanup-debris` or Binaryen/tool command-failure classes; Starshine emits invalid wasm; public preset-slot evidence requires ordered-neighborhood work; or a Binaryen source/lit refresh changes the contract. Future opportunities include switch/`br_table`, broader `br_on_*`, arbitrary ordered windows, local-copy dependency chains, richer `orderedBefore`, broader atomics/GC/EH/trap-option surfaces, and new aggregate leaves, but they are no longer active release blockers without fresh reopening evidence.

- [O4Z-AUDIT-CP-BINREP] - Finish `code-pushing` as a fuller Binaryen replacement
  - Status: completed 2026-06-25 in [`0901`](docs/wiki/raw/research/0901-2026-06-25-code-pushing-binrep-followup-closeout.md). The closeout in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0892-2026-06-25-code-pushing-final-closeout.md` remains valid for the old release-gating audit; this follow-up reduced additional Binaryen v130 behavior gaps where safe and documented the rest as narrow accepted boundaries with reopening criteria.
  - Goal: reduce remaining source-backed Binaryen-vs-Starshine `code-pushing` behavior gaps beyond the v0.1.0 closeout, using TDD and committing each atomic widening/boundary slice.
  - Why: Starshine should become a fuller Binaryen replacement, not merely clear the release-gating audit. Binaryen v130 `CodePushing.cpp` and lit suites show useful `optimizeIntoIf`, trap-mode, intrinsic-call, and GC/ref movement shapes that the closed audit intentionally left as future opportunities.
  - Replacement-oriented follow-up items:
    - [x] [CP-BINREP-001] Implement dependency-chain sinking into sole using `if` arms. Completed 2026-06-25 in [`0893`](docs/wiki/raw/research/0893-2026-06-25-code-pushing-dependency-chain-into-if.md): Starshine now sinks consecutive SFA local-copy dependency chains into the sole consuming void-`if` arm, preserving source order and rejecting source/moved-local arm writes. Red-first focused positive failed before implementation (`expected nop`), then focused `code_pushing_test.mbt` passed `123/123`, `moon fmt`, `moon test src/passes` passed `2832/2832`, native `src/cmd` build passed, `moon info` passed with pre-existing warnings, and bounded `code-pushing-all` smoke `.tmp/pass-fuzz-code-pushing-all-1000-20260625-binrep001-local-cleanup-rerun2` with `--normalize local-cleanup-debris` compared `1000/1000` with `466` normalized, `534` cleanup-normalized, raw mismatches/failures `0`. An earlier smoke using absent `target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe` produced only `ENOENT` Starshine command failures and is superseded by the `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe` rerun.
    - [x] [CP-BINREP-002] Honor Binaryen trap-relaxed movement for `--traps-never-happen` / `code-pushing_tnh.wast`. Completed 2026-06-25 in [`0895`](docs/wiki/raw/research/0895-2026-06-25-code-pushing-tnh-movement.md): Starshine now plumbs `HotPipelineOptions.traps_never_happen` into public `HotPassContext`, keeps exact trapping integer div/rem values before `if` by default, and sinks the reduced `i32.div_s` set into the sole using arm when `traps_never_happen=true`. Red-first positive failed before implementation, then focused `code_pushing_test.mbt` passed `125/125`, `moon fmt`, `moon info`, `moon test src/passes` passed `2834/2834`, native `src/cmd` build passed, and bounded `code-pushing-all` smoke `.tmp/pass-fuzz-code-pushing-all-1000-20260625-binrep002-tnh` with `--normalize local-cleanup-debris` compared `1000/1000` with `466` normalized, `534` cleanup-normalized, raw mismatches/failures `0`.
    - [x] [CP-BINREP-003] Audit and implement the Binaryen `code-pushing_ignore-implicit-traps.wast` surface or document the exact Starshine flag mapping/non-goal. Initially resolved 2026-06-25 as an accepted current-boundary/non-goal in [`0897`](docs/wiki/raw/research/0897-2026-06-25-code-pushing-ignore-implicit-traps-boundary.md), then superseded for implementation status by [`0902`](docs/wiki/raw/research/0902-2026-06-25-code-pushing-ignore-implicit-traps-implementation.md): Starshine now exposes distinct `--ignore-implicit-traps` / `-iit` support, keeps it separate from `--traps-never-happen` / TNH, and implements the lit `value-might-interfere` memory-load movement only under that flag.
    - [x] [CP-BINREP-004] Support Binaryen intrinsic no-effects calls where appropriate. Resolved 2026-06-25 as an accepted current-boundary/API blocker in [`0899`](docs/wiki/raw/research/0899-2026-06-25-code-pushing-intrinsic-no-effects-boundary.md): Binaryen `code-pushing_into_if.wast` sinks `binaryen-intrinsics/call.without.effects` calls when arguments are safe, but Starshine's `HotModuleContext` exposes function types rather than import module/name identity, and `HotOp::Call` carries only the function index. Do not implement a type/arity heuristic; reopen when pass-visible import identity can recognize the exact intrinsic and add red-first lit-derived `sink-call`, post-if-use, and `local.tee` argument tests.
    - [x] [CP-BINREP-005] Audit GC/ref into-if and `br_on` lit surfaces. Resolved 2026-06-25 as a mixed coverage/boundary closeout in [`0900`](docs/wiki/raw/research/0900-2026-06-25-code-pushing-gc-ref-boundary.md): existing direct-pass work covers Starshine-representable `RefFunc`, `br_on_*`, and atomics/GC non-null `struct.get` families, while Binaryen `code-pushing_into_if.wast` `ref-into-if` remains a current boundary because it requires local type weakening/refinalization after sinking changes non-null local dominance. Reopen with a local-refinalization helper, official shared-GC fixture support, or a generated/direct mismatch attributable to missing GC/ref movement.
    - [x] [CP-BINREP-006] Revisit broader `optimizeIntoIf` multi-set ordering after dependency-chain support. Completed 2026-06-25 in [`0896`](docs/wiki/raw/research/0896-2026-06-25-code-pushing-independent-into-if-order.md): the consecutive into-if multi-set helper now handles independent sets as well as dependency chains, preserving source order for the reduced independent two-set probe instead of letting fixed-point single-set sinking reverse the arm prefix. Red-first order test failed before implementation (`2 != 1`), then focused `code_pushing_test.mbt` passed `126/126`, `moon fmt`, `moon test src/passes` passed `2835/2835`, native `src/cmd` build passed, and bounded `code-pushing-all` smoke `.tmp/pass-fuzz-code-pushing-all-1000-20260625-binrep006-order` with `--normalize local-cleanup-debris` compared `1000/1000` with `466` normalized, `534` cleanup-normalized, raw mismatches/failures `0`.
    - [x] [CP-BINREP-007] Keep low-priority probes for multi-value `br_if` and `br_table` switch movement. Resolved 2026-06-25 as a narrow boundary closeout in [`0898`](docs/wiki/raw/research/0898-2026-06-25-code-pushing-branch-switch-boundary-closeout.md): existing notes `0824` and `0825` already implemented the useful branch-value `br_if` positives, while `0822`, `0843`, and `0848` protect the probed simple, value-carrying, and multi-label `br_table` shapes as Binaryen-stationary. Reopen only with a reduced Binaryen-positive switch/branch case, source/lit drift, or generated compare mismatch attributable to missing branch/switch movement.
  - Suggested sources: Binaryen v130 `src/passes/CodePushing.cpp`; Binaryen v130 lit tests `code-pushing_into_if.wast`, `code-pushing_tnh.wast`, `code-pushing_ignore-implicit-traps.wast`, `code-pushing-gc.wast`, `code-pushing-atomics.wast`, `code-pushing-eh.wast`, and `code-pushing-eh-legacy.wast`; Starshine `src/passes/code_pushing.mbt`, `src/passes/code_pushing_test.mbt`, and `src/passes/code_pushing_wbtest.mbt`.
  - Closeout validation decision: `0901` documents why the smaller matrix is sufficient for this replacement-follow-up closeout: the old four-lane direct-pass closeout remains in `0892`, each behavior-changing binrep slice ran red-first focused tests plus a bounded `code-pushing-all` smoke with `0` raw mismatches/failures, and the remaining binrep slices were docs/status boundary resolutions only.

- [O4Z-AUDIT-TO] - Deep audit `tuple-optimization`
  - Status: completed v0.1.0 release-gating `-O4z` per-pass audit as of 2026-06-30 with an evidence-backed soft performance exception. User caveat accepted: reasonable TO-owned optimizations were exhausted and final signoff/residual classification is recorded in [`1387`](docs/wiki/raw/research/1387-2026-06-30-tuple-optimization-closeout-soft-exception.md).
  - Scope: multivalue spill/copy splitting, passthrough chains, branch-exit carriers, local compaction, interaction with SLNS/RL/RUB, and candidate-heavy function runtime.
  - Deliverables: complete. Dedicated tuple carrier profiles exist and carry `profile_case_label` metadata; direct compare lanes and available exact-slot neighborhood evidence are refreshed; residual host-lane/local-map/scalar-spelling invariants and reopening criteria are documented.

Red-first validate profile tests failed on missing constructors, then focused validate/fuzz manifest tests passed. Initial count-30 dedicated profile smoke generated/validated all cases but produced `30/30` normalized mismatches; a follow-up count-6 post-label smoke recorded `profile_case_label` counts and also produced `6/6` normalized mismatches, so this profile is now the audit-driver for reducing active TO scalarization parity gaps; see [`1358`](docs/wiki/raw/research/1358-2026-06-29-tuple-optimization-genvalid-profile.md).

Focused tuple pass tests, registry smoke, `moon test src/passes`, and a native raw-carrier regression pass, but the count-30 `tuple-optimization-all` smoke still reports `30/30` normalized mismatches; residual scalar temp/tee/copy drift is open pending measured Starshine-win classification or further parity alignment. See [`1359`](docs/wiki/raw/research/1359-2026-06-29-tuple-optimization-typeidx-spill-scalarization.md).

- [O4Z-AUDIT-SLNS] - Deep audit `simplify-locals-nostructure`
  - Status: closed for `version_130` with an explicit user-accepted pass-local performance caveat; 2026-06-30 source baseline refreshed to local Binaryen `wasm-opt version 130 (version_130)` in [`1399`](docs/wiki/raw/research/1399-2026-06-30-slns-v130-source-refresh-and-tee-gap.md). Reopen for any Starshine validation/generator/property/semantic mismatch, new non-annotation residual, repeatable central-tendency pass-local regression above `2x`, or a concrete safe scan-region/tail optimization owner.
  - Scope: no-structure local sinking, no-tee variant spelling, dead write cleanup, interaction with tuple/reorder/vacuum, and raw gate behavior.
  - Current slice: direct-call barrier and nullable-local null-trap coverage refreshed after the dynamic read-only-load, distinct-global, try_table EH, overwritten pending-set, and tee-family slices. Canonical SLNS no longer bails out of straight-line/no-control existing-tee functions or root dropped-tee plus later-control functions; the no-tee sibling no longer bails out on the straight-line existing dropped-tee/unrelated-local shape. Binaryen `version_130` probes `.tmp/slns-overwrite-load-load.opt.wat` and `.tmp/slns-overwrite-load-load-notee.opt.wat` show both variants converting the first overwritten pending load set into `drop(i32.load ...)` and sinking the later load to the result; Starshine covers that straight-line load/trap-shaped family for both canonical and no-tee variants. Binaryen probes `.tmp/slns-try-table-nonthrowing-sink.opt.wat` and `.tmp/slns-try-table-throwing-barrier.opt.wat` show pure pending values may sink into a `try_table` body while call-valued pending sets that may throw stay before the handler; Starshine now clears only throwing sinkables before body scanning and keeps catch-list scanning fresh. Effect probes under `.tmp/slns-effect-audit/` show `global.get $g` sinks across `global.set $h` but not across `global.set $g`; Starshine now tracks exact global reads/writes for pending sinkables and covers both focused cases. Dynamic read-only loads commute while same-table and distinct-table `table.get` before `table.set` both remain Binaryen-stationary barriers; Starshine moves one-use `Load` values only for parameter-reading address subtrees, preserves dependent-load tee and atomic-read barriers, and covers table same/distinct negatives. Latest call/null probes under `.tmp/slns-call-audit/` keep const/load carriers across direct and imported calls, including a locally-defined pure-looking direct callee; `.tmp/slns-null-audit/ref-as-non-null-load-nullable-local.opt.wat` sinks a nullable-local `ref.as_non_null` carrier across a dropped read-only load. Starshine already matched those refreshed call/null boundaries, so the new focused tests are green coverage refreshes, not red-first fixes. Focused SLNS is now `28/28`, `moon test src/passes` is `3677/3677`, previous simplify-locals white-box was `4/4`, bounded GenValid smokes are green at `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-slice-genvalid-200`, `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-tee-guard2-genvalid-500`, `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-trytable-barrier-genvalid-500`, `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-global-effects-genvalid-500`, and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-load-table-effects-genvalid-500` using `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe`; focused filters for the new call/null tests pass. The dedicated GenValid aggregate `simplify-locals-nostructure-all` now exists with straight-line, tee/control, and effect/order leaves, and focused generator/manifest tests pass; its first 100-case smoke `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-genvalid-all-smoke-100` compared `100/100`, normalized `71`, mismatches `29`, and had zero validation/generator/command failures. Follow-up tracing classified all 29 aggregate residuals as effect-order cases hitting `skip-raw reason=root-local-set-stack-hazard-noop`; leaf smokes `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-straight-line-smoke-100` and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-tee-control-smoke-100` are `100/100` normalized, while `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-effect-order-smoke-30` is `30/30` mismatches for the same raw stack/local-set call-hazard guard. This narrowed the first active profile blocker to the raw/HOT root stack-effect guard; a 2026-06-30 follow-up then reduced that initial guard to call-valued local writes while preserving the broader lowered-cleanup guard for the historical json-as stack-root `local.tee` artifact. Red-first coverage `simplify-locals-nostructure optimizes pure local traffic before root calls` failed before implementation and now passes; focused SLNS is `29/29`, `moon test src/passes` is `3678/3678`, `moon test` is `7063/7063`, `moon info` passes, and native `src/cmd` builds with pre-existing warnings. The dedicated profile improved but is not green: `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-effect-order-smoke-30-after-narrow-stack-guard` is `20/30` normalized with zero failures and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-genvalid-all-smoke-100-after-narrow-stack-guard` is `91/100` normalized with zero failures; trace replay shows no remaining skip reasons. A follow-up classifier now shows all 10 leaf residuals and all 9 aggregate residuals are structured-control result-annotation-only output drift (`same_after_erasing_result_annotations=True`), and both Binaryen and Starshine optimized wasm artifacts validate. This exact smoke residual family is behavior-parity match despite output/refinalization drift, not broad nondefaultable/refinalization closure. A 2026-06-30 follow-up closed the narrow adjacent call-valued overwritten-set/final-use family for canonical and no-tee variants: Binaryen probes `.tmp/slns-call-overwrite-audit/overwrite-call-call.opt.wat` and `overwrite-call-call-notee.opt.wat` produce `drop(call $a)`, `nop`, `call $b`; red-first Starshine tests for both variants failed before implementation and now pass after narrowing the initial root stack/local-set guard to stack-effect-valued local writes that have a later stack-effecting read before the next write, plus allowing immediate following root `local.get` replacement for non-inline-safe single-result values. Focused call-overwrite `2/2`, focused hazard `2/2`, focused SLNS `31/31`, `moon test src/passes` `3680/3680`, `moon test` `7065/7065`, `moon info`, `moon fmt`, and native `src/cmd` build pass with pre-existing warnings. The post-call-overwrite dedicated smokes are unchanged and zero-failure: `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-effect-order-smoke-30-after-call-overwrite` is `20/30` normalized, and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-genvalid-all-smoke-100-after-call-overwrite` is `91/100` normalized with selected profiles `49/22/29`; treat these as the already-classified annotation-only residual family unless larger lanes show new drift. A follow-up late-equivalent/no-structure slice added direct copy/store/load get-canonicalization coverage and found/fixed the branch-free void-block sink gap: Binaryen sinks a local carrier through a plain fallthrough `block` without synthesizing a block result, while loops, one-armed ifs, and branch-created blocks keep void control plus local carriers. Red-first `simplify-locals-nostructure sinks across fallthrough block without block result` failed before implementation and now passes after Starshine keeps sinkables live across void branch-free blocks only; focused equivalent `1/1`, synthesize `4/4`, SLNS focused `36/36`, `moon test src/passes` `3687/3687`, `moon fmt`, native `src/cmd` build, and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-genvalid-all-smoke-100-after-block-fallthrough` (`91/100` normalized, zero failures, same classified residual IDs) pass. A later first-cycle/later-cycle tee refresh probed Binaryen canonical and no-tee multi-use shapes under `.tmp/slns-tee-cycle-audit/`: canonical SLNS creates `local.tee` on the later cycle, no-tee keeps the carrier, and a dropped-first-use shape avoids tee creation entirely. Starshine already matched, so the two new canonical tests are green coverage refreshes; focused `*tee*` passed `14/14`, focused SLNS `38/38`, `moon fmt`, and `moon test src/passes` `3689/3689` pass. A final dead-set cleanup breadth refresh probed Binaryen under `.tmp/slns-dead-set-audit/`: pure zero-read sets disappear, trapping load-valued sets become dropped loads, call-valued sets become dropped calls, and canonical copy-chain cleanup removes equivalent dead carriers; Starshine matches canonical and intentionally performs a narrower stronger no-tee pure-copy cleanup without creating tees or structure. Added green coverage tests for those shapes; focused `*zero-read*` passed `6/6`, focused `*dead copy*` passed `2/2`, `moon fmt`, focused SLNS `46/46`, `moon test src/passes` `3697/3697`, and `git diff --check` pass. A narrower `control-local-tee-hazard-noop` guard remains only for `local.tee` nodes embedded under structured control; broader stack-effect local-write hazards remain open for `call_ref` / `call_indirect`, non-adjacent call carriers, and shapes with later stack-effecting reads; plain legacy `try` body-barrier coverage has direct-HOT pure-vs-call tests because the focused WAT path desugars it before HOT `Try` coverage. The EH catch-region/catch-list slice probed Binaryen under `.tmp/slns-eh-catch-audit/`: pre-`try` const/call carriers stay outside catch bodies, while catch-local traffic still optimizes. Red-first direct-HOT Starshine tests exposed the following-root inliner crossing into `Try` / `TryTable` child slot `1`; after fixing the inliner to skip catch/catch-list child slots, focused `*catch*` passes `4/4`, `simplify_locals_wbtest.mbt` passes `10/10`, focused SLNS remains `46/46`, `moon test src/passes` passes `3701/3701`, native `src/cmd` builds, and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-genvalid-all-smoke-100-after-eh-catch` remains `91/100` normalized with zero failures and the same annotation-only residual IDs. A follow-up unreachable/refinalization slice probed Binaryen under `.tmp/slns-nondefaultable-audit/`: both canonical and no-tee no-structure remove a zero-read stack-style `local.set` after `unreachable`, while Binaryen also sinks an initialized nondefaultable `(ref $s)` struct local to the result and leaves a validating unused nondefaultable local declaration. Red-first Starshine coverage `simplify-locals-nostructure removes zero-read stack set after unreachable` failed before implementation and now passes after a no-structure-only raw cleanup in `pass_manager.mbt`; no-tee coverage also passes. Direct nondefaultable locals remain Starshine validation/tooling-blocked (`locals_error: local type has no default value`), and bottom set/get still has Binaryen tee-form vs Starshine stack-form unreachable-code drift. Focused `*zero-read*` passes `8/8`, focused SLNS passes `48/48`, `moon fmt`, `moon test src/passes` `3703/3703`, native `src/cmd` build, and `.tmp/pass-fuzz-slns-genvalid-all-smoke-100-after-unreachable-dead-set` (`91/100` normalized, zero failures, same annotation-only residual IDs) pass. Results: `242/300` repeat runs within `<=2x`, pass-ratio median/mean/p75/max `1.7930x` / `1.7871x` / `1.9412x` / `2.7598x`; per-case median ratio median/mean/p75/max `1.8043x` / `1.7879x` / `1.8837x` / `2.3251x`; `57/100` cases passed all three repeats, `86/100` passed at least two repeats, `14/100` passed fewer than two, and `1/100` passed none. The ten originally slow cases had `26/30` passing repeats, while originally within-floor cases produced new above-floor repeats. Classification: central tendency is within the ordinary floor, but tail ratios remain above-floor and noisy; this supports a bounded exception discussion but is not strict performance closeout, not a safe code fix, and not final performance acceptance.

All 100 sampled cases select `simplify-locals-nostructure-effect-order`; the `14/100` weak-tail cases have median case-level Starshine/Binaryen/main/non-main pass times of `279.5us` / `133.9us` / `126.0us` / `145.5us`, while the stronger `86/100` cases have `237.0us` / `133.9us` / `115.0us` / `121.0us`. Classification: this reinforces fixed-shape, sub-millisecond effect-order tail/noise evidence and finds no safe speculative code fix; performance acceptance/exception remains open, and a real fix still needs temporary or low-overhead subphase counters for scan/count/delete/writeback/dead-cleanup.

- [O4Z-AUDIT-H2L] - Deep audit `heap2local`
  - Status: completed for the current v0.1.0 direct-pass/`-O4z` audit scope on 2026-07-02. The audit added and documented the dedicated `heap2local-all` GenValid aggregate with `heap2local-struct`, `heap2local-array`, and `heap2local-ref` leaves; fixed generated repeated same-owner struct allocation epochs, repeated same-owner fixed-array allocation epochs, and direct fresh-struct `ref.test` folds; and classified the remaining generated residual family as raw Starshine output-shape/local-debris wins after both tools remove generated H2L traffic. Required compare evidence with `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe`: regular GenValid `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap2local-genvalid-100000-20260702` compared/normalized `100000/100000` with zero failures; wasm-smith `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap2local-wasm-smith-10000-unreachable-normalized-20260702` compared `9956/10000` with `9955` normalized, one existing `unreachable-control-debris` compare-normalized residual, zero mismatches, and `44` Binaryen/oracle command failures; dedicated `heap2local-all` `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap2local-all-10000-full-residuals-after-array-reftest` compared `10000/10000` with `2474` normalized and `7526` classified raw residuals; broad `random-all-profiles` `.tmp/pass-fuzz-heap2local-genvalid-random-all-profiles-10000-20260702` compared `10000/10000` with `8772` normalized and `1228` classified H2L-leaf raw residuals. Final timing/slot evidence: `.tmp/h2l-passlocal-probe-dedicated-9` recorded generated-case pass-local medians `0.033ms` Starshine vs `0.068469ms` Binaryen; `.tmp/h2l-o4z-slot-evidence-20260702/slot-h2l-compare` replayed the current generated O4z direct H2L slot as exact canonical output with pass-local `67.539ms` Starshine vs `153.397ms` Binaryen; H2L+optimize-casts+local-subtyping stayed exact, and the later coalesce-locals/local-cse size gap is neighbor-owned ordered-pipeline evidence unless future work ties it back to H2L output. Reopen if generated H2L ops appear in either output of a residual, Starshine stops being smaller for the classified local-debris family, validation/property failures appear, Binaryen source/lit drift adds an unimplemented required H2L family, neighbor evidence proves H2L caused the later coalesce/local-cse gap, or pass-local timing regresses outside the repo floor. Durable evidence lives in `docs/wiki/binaryen/passes/heap2local/fuzzing.md`, `docs/wiki/binaryen/passes/heap2local/parity.md`, and `docs/wiki/raw/research/1402-2026-07-02-heap2local-genvalid-profile-start.md`.
  - Scope closed: non-escaping generated struct locals, scalar field locals, fixed-array lowering, direct fresh-reference folds, null/test comparison coverage in the current profile, direct-pass compare matrix, pass-local timing, and current generated O4z H2L slot/neighborhood evidence.
  - Explicit non-scope / reopening surfaces: validator-rejected nondefaultable-local repair, broader dynamic array indexes/sizes, escapes through calls/returns, mixed branch/select provenance, and neighbor-owned coalesce-locals/local-cse output-size gaps.

- [O4Z-AUDIT-CL] - Deep audit `coalesce-locals`
  - Status: completed for the current v0.1.0 direct `coalesce-locals` / checked O4z GC-local neighborhood audit as of 2026-07-04. Direct refresh fixed structured body-local reuse of dead fixed params plus conservative loop unused/unread/copy-through coalescing. Ordered startup-map replays fixed CL-owned non-loop structured `local.tee`, self-copy cleanup, bounded structured branch copy-chain forwarding, derived branch-carrier consume-forwarding, destination-read-after-source-write guarding, source-write/destination-read interference restoration, path-disjoint branch-result slot reuse, branch-aware structured effective-write marking, effective-copy weighting/copy-connected coloring order, loop adjacent/non-adjacent copy-through coalescing, structured ineffective-write subsets, immediate tee/drop debris, nested nonlocal block-escape live-write preservation, label-aware terminal/branch-continuation liveness, tail param reuse after ineffective dead writes, concrete-ref direct-`struct.get` packing, preferred-first GC-ref ordering, and structured-scalar slot-order preference. The checked O4z startup-map prefixes are measured Starshine raw/code-body size wins (`-20` raw bytes at `+ coalesce-locals`, `-18` at `+ local-cse`) while exact normalized/canonical text equality is not claimed; first diff remains `defined=3 abs=3`, and function `18` remains a smaller local residual (`+20` code-body bytes) inside an aggregate Starshine-smaller code section. Dense non-loop coloring is bounded by an intentional `>4096` flattened-local skip covered by a 4097-local boundary test. `[AUDIT006-D]` is locally closed by the source invariant comment; broader `[AUDIT006]` work remains outside CL. Final direct matrix evidence with `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe`: regular GenValid `.tmp/pass-fuzz-coalesce-locals-genvalid-100000-structured-scalar-order-final-20260704` compared/normalized `100000/100000`; dedicated `coalesce-locals-all` `.tmp/pass-fuzz-coalesce-locals-profile-10000-structured-scalar-order-final-20260704` compared/normalized `10000/10000` with leaves `straight-line=4290`, `structured=2885`, `loop-copy-through=2825`; random all-profiles `.tmp/pass-fuzz-coalesce-locals-random-all-profiles-10000-structured-scalar-order-final-20260704` compared/normalized `10000/10000` and sampled `ssa-nomerge-smoke=1250`, `heap2local-struct=538`, all `coalesce-locals-*` leaves, and other neighboring profiles with zero failures; explicit wasm-smith raw `.tmp/pass-fuzz-coalesce-locals-wasm-smith-10000-structured-scalar-order-final-20260704` compared `9956/10000`, normalized `9955`, with one documented no-local unreachable-control cleanup-debris mismatch and `44` Binaryen/oracle command failures (`rec-group-zero=39`, `invalid-tag-index=1`, `table-index-out-of-range=1`, `bad-section-size=3`); cleanup-normalized `.tmp/pass-fuzz-coalesce-locals-wasm-smith-10000-structured-scalar-order-final-normalized-20260704` converted the debris to one compare-normalized match and left zero mismatches. Validation passed `moon info`, `moon fmt`, focused coalesce tests `35/35`, `moon test src/passes` `4017/4017`, full `moon test` `7441/7441`, native `src/cmd` build with pre-existing warnings, startup-map repro validation, and `git diff --check`.
  - Scope: closed for current direct pass parity, checked ordered O4z GC/local neighborhood, runtime artifact safety, dense-local GC-churn bounding, `[AUDIT006-D]` local invariant closure, and docs/backlog status. Reopen only for a new validation/runtime failure, unclassified direct compare mismatch, size-losing ordered CL neighborhood without measured Starshine benefit, release-significant huge-function missed coalescing above the 4096 guard, or source-backed need for sparse coloring/preset placement beyond the current evidence.
  - Deliverables: [x] apply the common checklist; [x] keep `[AUDIT006-D]` locally closed and hand broader invariant docs to `[AUDIT006]`; [x] cover interference/ref/name fixtures exposed by the audit; [x] keep the startup-map CL prefixes at the measured Starshine size-win state; [x] finish closeout matrix including broad `random-all-profiles`; [x] keep the documented `coalesce-locals-all` dedicated profile green; [x] keep the dense-coloring guard documented and reopen only for sparse-coloring or release-significant huge-function evidence.

- [O4Z-AUDIT-CF] - Deep audit and widen `code-folding` to Binaryen behavior parity
  - Status: completed v0.1.0 release-gating `-O4z` per-pass audit and implementation track; 2026-06-04 audit evidence lives in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0713-2026-06-04-code-folding-o4z-pass-audit.md`.
  - Goal: make Starshine `code-folding` semantically implement Binaryen's `CodeFolding.cpp` transform families, not merely remain a correct narrow subset. Byte-for-byte wasm/text parity is not required, but direct `--pass code-folding` output must be semantically equivalent to Binaryen across the standard compare lane, and remaining representation/code-size differences must be classified with evidence.
  - Scope: expression-exit tail sharing for named block exits and foldable `if` arms; function-ending terminating-tail sharing for `return`, `return_call*`, and `unreachable`; unsupported branch poisoning; branch-target/EH movement safety; helper labels/blocks; profitability/fixpoint behavior; late cleanup neighborhood; and pass-local runtime.
  - Current finding: source/test review found active owner, registry, dispatcher, and CLI coverage in place for a narrowed HOT pass. Focused coverage now includes full-`if` `unreachable` terminal suffix sharing, unsupported `br_on_null` label poisoning, live-label structured suffix bailouts, single-result typed block-exit branch-payload sharing with fallthrough or other plain-`br` payloads, a source-backed single-result multi-root named-block suffix slice that shares a matching void/effectful root before the final branch/fallthrough value root, and a source-backed multi-value branch-plus-fallthrough payload suffix. At that early checkpoint this was not yet Binaryen behavior parity; later CF slices below close the direct behavior gaps. Baseline slice `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-A]` is green: `moon info`, `moon fmt`, `moon test src/passes` (`1590/1590`), native `src/cmd` build at `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe`, direct 1000-case compare (`998` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, `2` `binaryen-rec-group-zero` command failures), timing-only debug-WASI replay (`172.276ms` Starshine pass vs `169.576ms` Binaryen pass, within <=2x), and full `moon test` (`4775/4775`). Follow-up `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-B]` through `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-D]` evidence is also green: test-first `moon test src/passes` failed the two new multi-root tests before implementation; after implementation `moon test src/passes` passed `1592/1592`, full `moon test` passed `4777/4777`, and `moon info` completed; native direct 1000-case compare at `.tmp/pass-fuzz-code-folding-bd-1000` had `998` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `2` `binaryen-rec-group-zero` command failures; timing-only debug-WASI replay at `.tmp/code-folding-bd-self-compare` was `196.213ms` Starshine pass vs `187.281ms` Binaryen pass, within <=2x. Late-slot replay, 10000-case closeout, and broader Binaryen behavior parity remain open. `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-E]` has concrete progress after the safe one-block/one-non-block and two-unnamed-block `if` value-suffix slices and now includes a HOT-level unreachable-condition bailout; exact unreachable-condition fixtures remain blocked by local HOT/lower support for bottom-typed conditions. `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-F]` now has a root-anchored helper-label terminating-tail slice beyond the earlier adjacent no-else `if` case: non-adjacent `return` tails and block-backed `unreachable` tails can share a deeper common suffix through a fresh wrapper label while preserving unique fallthrough prefixes. `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-G]` has started with the same root-anchored helper-label model for typed-result direct `return_call`, `return_call_indirect`, and core-built `return_call_ref` tails. Latest focused continuation validation used serialized per-slice `moon fmt` plus `moon test src/passes` and is green through `1647/1647`; the earlier five-slice closeout retained `moon info`, full `moon test` (`4822/4822`), native `src/cmd` build at `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe`, and native direct 1000-case compare at `.tmp/pass-fuzz-code-folding-five-slices-2026-06-05-1000` with `998/1000` compared cases, `998` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `2` `binaryen-rec-group-zero` command failures. Pass-local timing was not refreshed in this coverage batch; the previous timing-only debug-WASI replay at `.tmp/code-folding-hij-self-compare` was within <=2x. `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-H]` now has focused `br_table`/outside-target/switch-scope negatives, `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-I]` now preserves tested `try_table` terminal/block-exit bailout shapes while allowing only non-terminal EH-body `if` suffix folds, and `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-J]` now runs the current local root-anchored implementation to an intra-pass fixpoint and has a small late-neighborhood fixture. A requested pre-slice 10000-case compare smoke used the actual native binary at `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe` and reached `6768/10000` compared cases with `6768` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `20` tool/Binaryen command failures; the requested `target/native/...` binary path failed earlier with ENOENT and is recorded separately in the research note. New follow-up coverage now includes a simple operand-only return-tail profitability negative, simple result `return_call` and `return_call_indirect` sibling-tail profitability negatives, a late-neighborhood guard for the simple direct tail-call bailout, and a `catch_ref` `try_table` terminal-tail EH bailout matching Binaryen's preserved shapes. The invalid-output continuation then fixed Starshine command/validation failure families by preserving needed bottom tails, refusing multi-result dead-block flattening, using function return arity for branches to the implicit function label in HOT lift, and clearing the validator stack on repeated unreachable/branch escapes; focused validation passed `moon test src/passes` (`1658/1658`) and `moon test src/validate` (`1552/1552`), and final docs/code signoff passed `moon info`, `moon fmt`, and full `moon test` (`4843/4843`) after refreshing the local Moon registry cache with `moon update`. The latest 100000-case direct smoke at `.tmp/pass-fuzz-code-folding-100000-after-td-fixes` compared `99747/100000` cases with `99742` normalized matches, `0` Starshine command failures, `0` validation failures, `253` tool/Binaryen command failures, and `5` agent-classified semantic-safe representation/cleanup mismatches (`012741`/`043481`, `023083`, `082547`, and `046375`). The follow-up `046375` cleanup made that family size-winning (`73B` Starshine vs `74B` Binaryen) rather than size-losing; a 1000-case smoke stayed green (`998` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, `2` tool/Binaryen command failures), but the requested debug-WASI timing lane is outside the pass-local floor (`5051.616ms` Starshine pass vs `185.490ms` Binaryen pass). The next EH-body breadth slice then fixed the open throwing-body `catch_ref` / `catch_all_ref` non-terminal `if` suffix gap with failing-first local tests; `moon info`, `moon fmt`, and full `moon test` passed (`4846/4846`), native `src/cmd` rebuilt at `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe`, and direct 1000-case compare at `.tmp/pass-fuzz-code-folding-eh-catch-ref-body-1000` had `998` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `2` tool/Binaryen command failures. The next `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-E]` one-block/one-non-block `if` breadth slices matched Binaryen's partial value suffix fold when both arms have distinct prefixes, the full one-block value-arm fold to `drop(condition)` plus one suffix, and then full tiny/larger one-block void-arm folds to `drop(condition)` plus the shared void suffix; the next source-backed two-block void-arm suffix slice hoists a shared `call $sink` / `i32.const 7; drop` suffix out of two unused block arms while leaving each unique prefix in place; the latest embedded-select continuation folds a partial value `if` child by wrapping the demoted void `if` plus shared suffix in a typed result block, after the prior full multi-root non-block value-arm slice folded top-level `if` arms to `drop(condition)` plus the shared `nop` / value suffix and folded the same full shape as a `select` child; focused pass tests are green at `moon test src/passes` (`1694/1694`) after another fifteen source-backed embedded value-parent slices: first `br` payload, `unary`, `convert`, `load`, and `compare`; then `ref.is_null`, `table.get`, `table.set` index, `call_indirect`, and `memory.grow`; and now `table.grow`, `table.fill`, `memory.fill`, `memory.copy`, and `memory.init` partial value-`if` suffixes; final `moon fmt`, `moon info`, and full `moon test` passed (`4879/4879`), native `src/cmd` rebuilt at `_build/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe`, and direct 1000-case compare at `.tmp/pass-fuzz-code-folding-table-memory-bulk-1000` had `998` normalized matches, `0` mismatches, and `2` tool/Binaryen command failures. The code-folding audit is closed for v0.1.0 direct semantic parity: safe arbitrary non-root terminating-tail subsets are covered across return, unreachable, and tail-call terminators; non-EH movement safety was reopened and source-backed through internal-target `br_table` movement in `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-H]`; EH movement/repair/bailout behavior is classified in `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-I]`; `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-L]` is fixed/attributed with pass-local timing back inside the repo floor; `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-K]` accepts the five documented semantic-safe cleanup/representation mismatch families after the required 100000-case oracle; and exact helper cost/text-shape parity is documented as future non-semantic work rather than a release blocker.
  - Deliverables:
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-A]` Baseline the current widened implementation: run `moon info`, `moon fmt`, `moon test src/passes`, build native `src/cmd`, run direct `--pass code-folding --count 1000 --jobs auto --starshine-bin <actual cmd.exe>`, run timing-only `self-optimize-compare ... --code-folding`, and record exact binary path, compared count, command failures, mismatches, and pass-local Starshine/Binaryen timings in the research note.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-B]` Build a source-backed Binaryen shape matrix from `CodeFolding.cpp` and `test/lit/passes/code-folding.wast`: rows for named-block exits, `if` arms, terminating returns, terminating `return_call*`, terminating `unreachable`, unsupported-branch poison, outside-target movement bailouts, EH-sensitive bailouts/repair, helper/profitability cases, and fixpoint exposure. Matrix lives in `docs/wiki/raw/research/0713-2026-06-04-code-folding-o4z-pass-audit.md` with local tests, gaps, and next-slice owners.
    - [x] `[O4Z-AUDIT-CF-C]` Finish expression-exit candidate modeling for named block exits: `CodeFoldingValueExitTail` now carries owning region/tail pointer, branch/fallthrough kind, target label, branch payload roots, result arity, movement proof state, selected suffix length, and profitability score; unsupported `br_on_*` / `br_if` / `br_table` / `delegate` target traffic still poisons the fold.

- [O4Z-AUDIT-RSE] - Deep audit `redundant-set-elimination`
  - Status: behavior completed for the current direct-pass transform-family audit as of 2026-07-05, and the user-requested direct pass-local 1x Binaryen timing follow-up is complete on the established `.tmp/rse-timing/` fixtures. The reopened loop/default/fixed-point precision gap from the 2026-07-04 matrix is fixed for the official Binaryen all-features source/test residuals, and the matrix has no unclassified RSE-owned behavior gaps.
  - Scope implemented: Binaryen-style same-value `local.set` / `local.tee` shell removal, local-copy/self-set identities including unknown-value `local.get` materialization, branch/default facts, loop untouched-local preservation, stable-entry backedge probes, post-loop source agreement, refined strict-subtype `local.get` retargeting, GC wrapper/accessor retargeting including `struct.atomic.get`, exception/hazard barriers, and pass-owned GenValid coverage.
  - Closure evidence: focused red-first/default loop test failed before implementation; after the performance follow-up, `moon info`, `moon fmt`, focused `moon test --target native src/passes/rse_test.mbt` (`39/39`), `moon test src/passes` (`4199/4199`), full `moon test` (`7628/7628`), and `moon build --target native --release src/cmd` passed. The official all-features replay diff is empty (`diff -u .tmp/rse-audit-binaryen/binaryen-rse-all-features.opt.wat .tmp/rse-audit-binaryen/starshine-rse-all-features.binaryenprint.wat` produced `0` lines). Final compare matrix with the rebuilt native binary: regular GenValid `.tmp/pass-fuzz-rse-genvalid-100000-closeout-20260705` compared `100000/100000` with zero mismatches/failures; dedicated profile `.tmp/pass-fuzz-rse-genvalid-profile-10000-closeout-20260705` compared `10000/10000` with zero mismatches/failures; random all-profiles `.tmp/pass-fuzz-rse-genvalid-random-all-profiles-10000-closeout-20260705` compared `10000/10000` with zero mismatches/failures; external wasm-smith `.tmp/pass-fuzz-rse-wasm-smith-10000-closeout-20260705` compared `9956/10000` with `9955` normalized matches, `44` Binaryen/tool command failures, and one raw generic `drop(unreachable)` debris mismatch from a no-local-candidate input. Supplementary `.tmp/pass-fuzz-rse-wasm-smith-10000-closeout-20260705-unreachable-normalized` with `--normalize unreachable-control-debris` classified that mismatch as compare-normalized and left `0` remaining mismatches. Historical retained evidence also includes mixed direct RSE `.tmp/pass-fuzz-rse-mixed`, dedicated `.tmp/pass-fuzz-rse-profile-4`, and direct `rse-vacuum-tail` `.tmp/pass-fuzz-rse-tail-rse-only`, each `10000/10000` with zero mismatches/failures.
  - Timing evidence: `docs/wiki/raw/research/1463-2026-07-05-rse-pass-timing.md` records traced direct-pass `pipeline` timing against `BINARYEN_PASS_DEBUG=1` Binaryen pass-local timing on `.tmp/rse-timing/` fixtures. The initial post-audit timing missed 1x by 3.53x/4.12x on the 1000-function straight/loop-heavy fixtures and 7.00x/6.42x on the 3000-function probes. The final i32 raw fast-path slice now meets the target: `.tmp/rse-timing/rse-i32coded-final-1000-summary.json` records 0.93x straight-heavy and 0.95x loop-heavy, and `.tmp/rse-timing/rse-i32coded-3000-summary.json` records 0.89x straight-heavy and 0.91x loop-heavy. The official all-features fixture remains too small/noisy for performance claims.
  - Follow-up boundary: direct `--pass rse --pass vacuum` on the RSE profile still exposes inherited Starshine/Binaryen `vacuum` representation/cleanup differences for live local.set debris; do not implement generic dead-store elimination in RSE to close that lane. Reopen RSE behavior only for a new Binaryen source/test family, generated true RSE-owned mismatch, validation/runtime failure, or regression in the classified transform-family matrix.
  - Performance follow-up: `[O4Z-AUDIT-RSE-PERF]` complete for the user-requested 1x Binaryen pass-local target on the established synthetic timing fixtures. The recursive performance slices replaced raw RSE `String` value payloads with integer ids, added numeric fast paths, avoided module-backed validation environments for numeric-only raw RSE functions, aggregated trace output, added RSE detail timers, combined i32 loop summary/stable-entry analysis, and added an i32-only raw fast path with bounded arithmetic ids for simple repeated i32 binary expressions. Validation passed focused RSE tests (`39/39`), `moon fmt`, native `src/cmd` build, and dedicated 1000-case RSE profile compare `.tmp/pass-fuzz-rse-perf-i32coded-smoke` (`1000/1000`, zero failures/mismatches). Final timings in `.tmp/rse-timing/rse-i32coded-final-1000-summary.json` record Starshine/Binaryen traced pass-local medians of 11.88/12.77 ms on `rse-straight-heavy-1000f.wasm` (0.93x) and 18.93/19.84 ms on `rse-loop-heavy-1000f.wasm` (0.95x). The larger `.tmp/rse-timing/rse-i32coded-3000-summary.json` probe also stays below Binaryen: 34.38/38.67 ms straight-heavy (0.89x) and 58.61/64.67 ms loop-heavy (0.91x). Do not implement generic dead-store elimination in RSE; reopen performance only if these fixtures regress above Binaryen or a representative direct RSE workload exposes a new pass-local owner.

## Deferred Until MoonBit Threading Support

- [HOT]002 - Native Parallel Hot-Batch Queue
  - Status: deferred; MoonBit does not currently support the threading model Starshine needs for a safe native worker queue.
  - Resume when: MoonBit native threading is stable enough to run per-function optimizer workers with deterministic, byte-stable output and explicit runtime gating.
  - Deliverables: add a native-only worker queue over eligible defined functions for the hot batch payload (`ssa-nomerge -> dead-code-elimination -> vacuum -> optimize-instructions -> simplify-locals`); keep final output byte-stable and deterministic; gate behind an explicit native-only option.
  - Dependencies: [HOT]001 replay hardening must stay green.

## v0.1.1 Backlog

### Optimizer Audit Follow-Ups

- [AUDIT]001 - Hot Pass Descriptor Metadata Truthfulness
  - Status: active follow-up from the 2026-05-31 optimizer audit. The former `global-refining` thin-coverage item is retired because the 2026-06-18 direct Binaryen behavior audit closed GR-001..GR-006 and published focused coverage plus 100000-case compare evidence.
  - Goal: make hot pass `requires` metadata describe every analysis a pass may request through `pass_require_*`, so the registry remains a truthful pass-author contract and future scheduling/perf tooling can trust descriptors.
  - Why: the audit found passes that lazily request analyses without listing them in `requires`; tests currently pass because the helper layer builds analyses on demand, but the metadata contract is stale.
  - Deliverables:
    - [x] `[AUDIT001-A]` Add focused descriptor tests for `optimize-instructions` requiring `use_def` and `effects`; confirm failure before metadata changes. Completed 2026-07-02 in `src/passes/registry_test.mbt`; the focused registry test failed red with `[] != [UseDef, Effects]` before the descriptor update.

Completed 2026-07-02 with `registry_test_assert_requires(...)` and the `audited pass_require helper metadata contracts stay declared` table covering OI, RUB, precompute-prefix, pick-load-signs, tuple-optimization, heap2local, and local-cse.

- [AUDIT]002 - Pass-Manager Raw Skip And Gate Boundary Coverage
  - Status: active follow-up from the 2026-05-31 optimizer audit.
  - Goal: protect large-function raw precleaner / skip heuristics from silent coverage holes and performance cliffs.
  - Why: `src/passes/pass_manager.mbt` contains many threshold-shaped gates for `remove-unused-brs`, `simplify-locals`, and related raw adapters. They are performance-motivated, but nearby shapes can miss cleanup or unexpectedly fall back to expensive HOT lowering. The 2026-06-04 `[O4Z-AUDIT-SL]` closeout retired the simplify-locals semantic/direct and generated late-neighborhood audit gate, but intentionally left small structured call-mesh and giant/no-structure raw threshold boundary coverage here under `[AUDIT002-F]` / `[AUDIT002-G]`.
  - Deliverables:
    - [ ] `[AUDIT002-A]` Inventory every `can_skip`, `unchecked`, `giant`, `large`, and threshold-based raw gate in `pass_manager.mbt`, grouping them by owning pass and intended artifact/function family.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT002-B]` Add white-box tests for `remove-unused-brs` large-result `br_table` dispatch ladder gates: exact match, locals/instruction lower-bound miss, block-depth miss, and HOT-only-candidate miss.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT002-C]` Add white-box tests for `remove-unused-brs` large value-if branch ladder gates: locals range, instruction range, call range, return/block range, and drop/select/br_if negatives.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT002-D]` Add white-box tests for `remove-unused-brs` drop-heavy / typed-`br_table` / void-if-return ladder gates, one predicate family per test so boundary failures identify the exact gate.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT002-E]` Add white-box tests for `remove-unused-brs` moderate and unchecked call-mesh gates, including ±1 thresholds around locals, instruction count, if count, and call count.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT002-F]` Add white-box tests for `simplify-locals` small structured call mesh gates: exact match, instruction lower/upper bounds, local-get/call ranges, and exact-tail negative.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT002-G]` Add white-box tests for `simplify-locals` giant validator and no-structure gates, with one fixture per threshold family rather than one mega-fixture.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT002-H]` For each broad skip family touched by `[AUDIT002-B]` through `[AUDIT002-G]`, add one public-pipeline fixture proving the intended cleanup either still runs or intentionally stays skipped with a trace reason.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT002-I]` Add trace strings for currently silent broad skip decisions where a future agent cannot tell whether a function was skipped for safety, performance, or shape mismatch.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT002-J]` Run a focused artifact timing replay for the debug artifact after adding trace/tests; document any gate that exists only to stay within `Starshine <= 2x Binaryen`.
  - Suggested tests: `moon test src/passes`, focused `pass_manager_wbtest`, and a timing-only `self-optimize-compare` replay only after asking if it will be long.
  - Exit criteria: every raw skip/gate family has boundary tests and a documented purpose; no threshold is an unexplained magic number.

- [AUDIT]003 - DAE Selected-Shape De-Artifacting And Genericization
  - Status: active v0.1.1 follow-up; not a v0.1.0 blocker because current DAE direct-pass surfaces are accepted.
  - Goal: convert remaining selected `FuncXXX` artifact lanes into generic, named, semantics-first shape recognizers where practical, while keeping accepted artifact evidence intact.
  - Why: the audit found many selected-function helpers in `dead_argument_elimination.mbt`. They are covered and historically justified, but they are hard to maintain and weak as a general optimizer surface.
  - Deliverables:
    - [ ] `[AUDIT003-A]` Inventory selected-function helpers and tests into a markdown table with columns: helper, selected def(s), fixture/test, transform family, safety guard, artifact reason, and current generic candidate.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT003-B]` Classify Func237 carrier/loop/local-map helpers only; decide which are genericizable and which are intentionally artifact-local.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT003-C]` Classify Func256/Func298 loop-carrier helpers only; decide which are genericizable and which are intentionally artifact-local.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT003-D]` Classify Func287/Func288 setup/switch-carrier helpers only; decide which are genericizable and which are intentionally artifact-local.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT003-E]` Classify Func299 inverted-if, Func311/Func313 terminal-call-arg, and Func408/Func505/Func521 literal/control helpers, one family at a time.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT003-F]` Pick the smallest safe classified family and add a reduced non-artifact fixture that fails without a generic recognizer and does not depend on absolute debug-artifact function indices.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT003-G]` Implement only that one family behind semantic guards, keep the selected artifact fixture as a regression, and remove only the corresponding selected-index gate if artifact/timing/fuzz evidence stays green.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT003-H]` Repeat `[AUDIT003-F]`/`[AUDIT003-G]` for the next smallest family; every repeat gets its own focused test-first commit-sized slice, not a broad DAE rewrite.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT003-I]` For families that remain selected-only, add comments in source and wiki explaining why they are intentionally artifact-local and what evidence would reopen them.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT003-J]` Refresh direct `--dae-optimizing` compare with DAE normalizers and classify any mismatch families by agent judgment, keeping accepted raw-cleanup drift separate from true semantic mismatches.
  - Suggested tests: focused DAE fixtures first, `moon test src/passes`, direct `bun scripts/pass-fuzz-compare.ts --pass dae-optimizing --normalize drop-consts --normalize unreachable-control-debris --jobs auto --starshine-bin target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe`, and artifact timing when selected lanes move.
  - Exit criteria: selected-function lanes are either replaced by generic recognizers or explicitly documented as intentionally artifact-local with current evidence.

- [AUDIT]004 - Thin Module-Pass Shape Coverage Expansion
  - Status: active follow-up from the 2026-05-31 optimizer audit.
  - Goal: add small, public-pipeline tests for module passes whose current direct coverage is thin relative to their implementation breadth.
  - Why: the all-pass fuzz smoke found no validation failures, but several module passes have only a small number of focused tests and are likely places for missing shapes to hide.
  - Deliverables:
    - [ ] `[AUDIT004-D]` `directize` table visibility negatives: add separate imported-table and exported-table tests proving indirect calls remain indirect.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT004-E]` `directize` element coverage: add passive element, declarative element, active nonconstant offset, active out-of-range hole, and type-mismatch-trap tests.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT004-F]` `directize` multi-table and tail-call coverage: add one multi-table partial optimization fixture, one `return_call_indirect` fixture, and one unsupported `call_ref`/reference-call non-interference fixture if WAT support exists.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT004-G]` `duplicate-import-elimination`: add separate tests for same module/name duplicate function imports, different module/name same-signature duplicates, nonfunction-import negatives, and different-signature negatives.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT004-H]` `duplicate-import-elimination` remapping: add separate call, `ref.func`, table element, export/start, and name/custom-section preservation tests.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT004-I]` `merge-locals`: add separate tests for multi-value functions, GC/ref locals with equal types, GC/ref locals with unequal types, dead-tail local traffic, write-interrupted copy chains, effect-interrupted copy chains, and local-name cleanup.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT004-L]` `once-reduction`: add separate tests for table escape, global escape, `ref.func` escape, export escape, start-function behavior, imported function negative, exported function negative, and multi-use once-positive.
    - [ ] `[AUDIT004-N]` After each pass-family test batch, run the pass-targeted compare smoke with `--count 1000` before moving to the next family; do not batch all behavior changes behind one signoff.
  - Suggested tests: adjacent `*_test.mbt` public-pipeline fixtures, `moon test src/passes`, and pass-targeted compare smoke for each touched pass.
  - Exit criteria: each listed module pass has positive and negative coverage for its most important module shapes, with no new validation failures or unexplained Binaryen mismatches.

- [AUDIT]005 - Standalone No-Inline Pass Contract Tests

  - Why: `src/passes/no_inline.mbt` has meaningful standalone behavior but no adjacent `no_inline_test.mbt` file.
